flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The withstanding of any material to rubbing, scratching, or wearing away.
flashcards.explanation_long: Abrasion: The withstanding of any material to rubbing, scratching, or wearing away.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The total pressure equal to that measured from an absolute vacuum. It equals the sum of gauge pressure plus barometric atmospheric pressure. It is expressed in pounds per square inch (psia) or kilopascals per square meter (kPa/m 2 ).
flashcards.explanation_long: Absolute pressure: The total pressure equal to that measured from an absolute vacuum. It equals the sum of gauge pressure plus barometric atmospheric pressure. It is expressed in pounds per square inch (psia) or kilopascals per square meter (kPa/m 2 ).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Temperature measured from absolute zero.
flashcards.explanation_long: Absolute temperature: Temperature measured from absolute zero.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The point at which any substance has no molecular motion and no heat. It is equivalent to 459.72°F or 273.18°C.
flashcards.explanation_long: Absolute zero: The point at which any substance has no molecular motion and no heat. It is equivalent to 459.72°F or 273.18°C.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The soaking up of a gas or liquid into a solid substance.
flashcards.explanation_long: Absorption: The soaking up of a gas or liquid into a solid substance.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A panel that can be opened and used to provide easy approach to concealed valves or equipment.
flashcards.explanation_long: Access door: A panel that can be opened and used to provide easy approach to concealed valves or equipment.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: That which enables a fixture, device, or appliance to be easily reached.
flashcards.explanation_long: Access to: That which enables a fixture, device, or appliance to be easily reached.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The degree of agreement between a measured value and the true value of a quantity or concern.
flashcards.explanation_long: Accuracy: The degree of agreement between a measured value and the true value of a quantity or concern.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: That which enables direct approach, either with or without the removing or moving of any panel, door, or similar obstruction. Regarding physically challenged individuals, a plumbing fixture, site, building, facility, or portion thereof that can be approached, entered, and/or used.
flashcards.explanation_long: Accessible: That which enables direct approach, either with or without the removing or moving of any panel, door, or similar obstruction. Regarding physically challenged individuals, a plumbing fixture, site, building, facility, or portion thereof that can be approached, entered, and/or used.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The amount a pressure, temperature, liquid level, or differential pressure deviates higher from a set value.
flashcards.explanation_long: Accumulation: The amount a pressure, temperature, liquid level, or differential pressure deviates higher from a set value.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A container in which fluid is stored under pressure as a source of power.
flashcards.explanation_long: Accumulator: A container in which fluid is stored under pressure as a source of power.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A fluid with a pH lower than 7.0.
flashcards.explanation_long: Acid: A fluid with a pH lower than 7.0.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe venting an acid-waste system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Acid vent: A pipe venting an acid-waste system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe that conveys liquid waste matter having a pH of less than 7.0.
flashcards.explanation_long: Acid waste: A pipe that conveys liquid waste matter having a pH of less than 7.0.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Sewage sediment, rich in destructive bacteria, that can be used to break down fresh sewage quickly.
flashcards.explanation_long: Activated sludge: Sewage sediment, rich in destructive bacteria, that can be used to break down fresh sewage quickly.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A movable component of a valve that when operated causes the closure element to move.
flashcards.explanation_long: Actuator: A movable component of a valve that when operated causes the closure element to move.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: With reference to inlet conditions, the volume rate of air compressed and delivered at the dis charge point.
flashcards.explanation_long: Actual capacity: With reference to inlet conditions, the volume rate of air compressed and delivered at the dis charge point.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any fitting that mates or connects two pipes or fittings of different size, material, or design.
flashcards.explanation_long: Adapter fitting: Any fitting that mates or connects two pipes or fittings of different size, material, or design.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A substance capable of holding two or more objects together by attaching to their surfaces.
flashcards.explanation_long: Adhesive: A substance capable of holding two or more objects together by attaching to their surfaces.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Air compression that allows an increase of temperature in the air.
flashcards.explanation_long: Adiabatic compression: Air compression that allows an increase of temperature in the air.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The surface retention or adhesion of a gas on the surface of a solid .
flashcards.explanation_long: Adsorption: The surface retention or adhesion of a gas on the surface of a solid .

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Also called the authority having jurisdiction, the individual official, board, department, entity, or agency established and authorized by a state, county, city, or other political subdivision created by law to administer and enforce the provisions of a particular code as adapted or amended.
flashcards.explanation_long: Administrative authority: Also called the authority having jurisdiction, the individual official, board, department, entity, or agency established and authorized by a state, county, city, or other political subdivision created by law to administer and enforce the provisions of a particular code as adapted or amended.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An artificial method of bringing a liquid into direct contact with air.
flashcards.explanation_long: Aeration: An artificial method of bringing a liquid into direct contact with air.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Living or active only in the presence of free oxygen.
flashcards.explanation_long: Aerobic: Living or active only in the presence of free oxygen.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A small vapor particle suspended in air.
flashcards.explanation_long: Aerosol: A small vapor particle suspended in air.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device used to lower the temperature of a gas after the compression process.
flashcards.explanation_long: Aftercooler: A device used to lower the temperature of a gas after the compression process.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The effect of exposing material to a specific environment for an extended period of time.
flashcards.explanation_long: Aging: The effect of exposing material to a specific environment for an extended period of time.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A one-way valve designed to open and allow air to enter a drainage system when negative pressures are present without extending to the outside air and then positively close by gravity.
flashcards.explanation_long: Air-admittance valve: A one-way valve designed to open and allow air to enter a drainage system when negative pressures are present without extending to the outside air and then positively close by gravity.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device, either made up of fittings or manufactured, installed on a piping system for the purpose of attenuating a pressure surge resulting from water hammer.
flashcards.explanation_long: Air chamber: A device, either made up of fittings or manufactured, installed on a piping system for the purpose of attenuating a pressure surge resulting from water hammer.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Air at any pressure greater than atmospheric pressure.
flashcards.explanation_long: Air, compressed: Air at any pressure greater than atmospheric pressure.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Air subject only to ambient atmospheric conditions.
flashcards.explanation_long: Air, free: Air subject only to ambient atmospheric conditions.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The unobstructed vertical distance between the end of a pipe and the flood level of a plumbing fixture or receptacle.
flashcards.explanation_long: Air gap: The unobstructed vertical distance between the end of a pipe and the flood level of a plumbing fixture or receptacle.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Sometimes called vapor lock, a condition in which a gas is entrapped between two liquid surfaces in a conduit causing a stoppage or impediment.
flashcards.explanation_long: Air lock: Sometimes called vapor lock, a condition in which a gas is entrapped between two liquid surfaces in a conduit causing a stoppage or impediment.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Air having a temperature of 60°F (15.6°C), with a pressure of 14.70 psia (101.4 kPa) and 0 percent humidity. These figures may be different outside the United States.
flashcards.explanation_long: Air, standard: Air having a temperature of 60°F (15.6°C), with a pressure of 14.70 psia (101.4 kPa) and 0 percent humidity. These figures may be different outside the United States.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A test using compressed air applied to a plumbing system for the purpose of determining a leak.
flashcards.explanation_long: Air test: A test using compressed air applied to a plumbing system for the purpose of determining a leak.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any audible or visible signal indicating the existence of any condition or operation outside a preset normal.
flashcards.explanation_long: Alarm: Any audible or visible signal indicating the existence of any condition or operation outside a preset normal.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A check valve, equipped with a signaling device, that will annunciate a remote alarm when a sprinkler head is discharging.
flashcards.explanation_long: Alarm check valve: A check valve, equipped with a signaling device, that will annunciate a remote alarm when a sprinkler head is discharging.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Alarm check valve

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A fluid with a pH of 7.0 or higher.
flashcards.explanation_long: Alkali: A fluid with a pH of 7.0 or higher.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A substance composed of two or more metals or a metal and nonmetal intimately united, usually fused together and dissolving in each other when molten.
flashcards.explanation_long: Alloy: A substance composed of two or more metals or a metal and nonmetal intimately united, usually fused together and dissolving in each other when molten.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A metallic pipe composed of two or more metals.
flashcards.explanation_long: Alloy pipe: A metallic pipe composed of two or more metals.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The average or mean temperature of the surrounding air where a reading is taken.
flashcards.explanation_long: Ambient temperature: The average or mean temperature of the surrounding air where a reading is taken.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A type of screw thread commonly used on pipe and fittings.
flashcards.explanation_long: American standard pipe thread: A type of screw thread commonly used on pipe and fittings.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | American standard pipe thread

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device that magnifies the input signal using power other than that from the signal itself.
flashcards.explanation_long: Amplifier: A device that magnifies the input signal using power other than that from the signal itself.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A measurement of the distance between the highest to the lowest excursion of a variable or physical motion.
flashcards.explanation_long: Amplitude: A measurement of the distance between the highest to the lowest excursion of a variable or physical motion.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: (re: bacteria) Living or active in the absence of free oxygen.
flashcards.explanation_long: Anaerobic: (re: bacteria) Living or active in the absence of free oxygen.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Separation and measurements of component parts.
flashcards.explanation_long: Analysis: Separation and measurements of component parts.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device used to fasten or secure pipes to a building or structure.
flashcards.explanation_long: Anchor: A device used to fasten or secure pipes to a building or structure.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: In a pipe, the angle between radial lines from the beginning and end of the bend to the center.
flashcards.explanation_long: Angle of bend: In a pipe, the angle between radial lines from the beginning and end of the bend to the center.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Angle of bend

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Common term for right-angle valves used to control water supplies to plumbing fixtures.
flashcards.explanation_long: Angle stop: Common term for right-angle valves used to control water supplies to plumbing fixtures.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device, usually of the globe type, in which the inlet and outlet are at right angles.
flashcards.explanation_long: Angle valve: A device, usually of the globe type, in which the inlet and outlet are at right angles.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A negatively charged atom attracted to the negative electrode.
flashcards.explanation_long: Anion: A negatively charged atom attracted to the negative electrode.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A procedure for preventing or removing stress within a material through controlled heating and/or cooling.
flashcards.explanation_long: Anneal: A procedure for preventing or removing stress within a material through controlled heating and/or cooling.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A term used to describe any device that eliminates siphonic action.
flashcards.explanation_long: Antisiphon: A term used to describe any device that eliminates siphonic action.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A term, expressed in degrees, indicating how close the outlet temperature of a fluid being heated in a heat exchanger comes to the temperature of the heating medium.
flashcards.explanation_long: Approach: A term, expressed in degrees, indicating how close the outlet temperature of a fluid being heated in a heat exchanger comes to the temperature of the heating medium.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Accepted or acceptable under an applicable specification or standard stated or cited for the proposed use under the procedures and authority of the administrative authority.
flashcards.explanation_long: Approved: Accepted or acceptable under an applicable specification or standard stated or cited for the proposed use under the procedures and authority of the administrative authority.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An organization established for purposes of testing products to approved standards and acceptable to the administrative authority.
flashcards.explanation_long: Approved testing agency: An organization established for purposes of testing products to approved standards and acceptable to the administrative authority.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Approved testing agency

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A water-bearing underground formation or stratum capable of storing water suitable for development.
flashcards.explanation_long: Aquifer: A water-bearing underground formation or stratum capable of storing water suitable for development.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A receptacle designed to collect surface or rainwater from a determined or calculated open area.
flashcards.explanation_long: Area drain: A receptacle designed to collect surface or rainwater from a determined or calculated open area.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An enclosed excavated area below grade adjacent to a building open to the weather.
flashcards.explanation_long: Areaway: An enclosed excavated area below grade adjacent to a building open to the weather.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A gas with little or no toxicity, but which could induce unconsciousness or death by replacing air, thus depriving an organism of oxygen.
flashcards.explanation_long: Asphyxiant gas: A gas with little or no toxicity, but which could induce unconsciousness or death by replacing air, thus depriving an organism of oxygen.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A fitting or device supplied with water or other fluid under positive pressure that passes through an integral orifice or “constriction,” causing a vacuum.
flashcards.explanation_long: Aspirator: A fitting or device supplied with water or other fluid under positive pressure that passes through an integral orifice or “constriction,” causing a vacuum.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An event that occurs at an arbitrary time without synchronization to a referenced clock.
flashcards.explanation_long: Asynchronous: An event that occurs at an arbitrary time without synchronization to a referenced clock.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A mechanical device consisting of a check valve that opens to the atmosphere when the pressure in the piping drops to atmospheric.
flashcards.explanation_long: Atmospheric vacuum breaker: A mechanical device consisting of a check valve that opens to the atmosphere when the pressure in the piping drops to atmospheric.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Atmospheric vacuum breaker

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The relative weight of an atom of an element compared to carbon 12.
flashcards.explanation_long: Atomic weight: The relative weight of an atom of an element compared to carbon 12.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The organization, office, or individual responsible for approving equipment, materials, installation, or procedure. 24 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
flashcards.explanation_long: Authority having jurisdiction (AHJ): The organization, office, or individual responsible for approving equipment, materials, installation, or procedure. 24 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Authority having jurisdiction (AHJ)

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The temperature at which a material will ignite and sustain combustion in the absence of a spark or flame.
flashcards.explanation_long: Autoignition: The temperature at which a material will ignite and sustain combustion in the absence of a spark or flame.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The closeness of a public sewer to a building based on code requirements, generally 500 ft (151 m) or less.
flashcards.explanation_long: Availability of a sewer: The closeness of a public sewer to a building based on code requirements, generally 500 ft (151 m) or less.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Availability of a sewer

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Material used to replace excavated material for piping installed in an earthen trench.
flashcards.explanation_long: Backfill: Material used to replace excavated material for piping installed in an earthen trench.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: 1. The flow of water or other liquids, mixtures, or substances into the distributing pipes of a potable supply from any source other than that intended. 2. The reversal of flow in drainage systems.
flashcards.explanation_long: Backflow: 1. The flow of water or other liquids, mixtures, or substances into the distributing pipes of a potable supply from any source other than that intended. 2. The reversal of flow in drainage systems.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A arrangement where backflow can occur.
flashcards.explanation_long: Backflow connection: A arrangement where backflow can occur.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device or means to prevent backflow into the potable water system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Backflow preventer: A device or means to prevent backflow into the potable water system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A metal strip used to prevent melted metal from the welding process from entering a pipe in the process of making a butt-welded joint.
flashcards.explanation_long: Backing ring: A metal strip used to prevent melted metal from the welding process from entering a pipe in the process of making a butt-welded joint.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Negative pressure created by any means in the potable water distribution system which causes a potential backflow.
flashcards.explanation_long: Backpressure: Negative pressure created by any means in the potable water distribution system which causes a potential backflow.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The flowing back of potentially contaminated or polluted water into the potable water supply piping due to a negative pressure in the potable water supply pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Backsiphonage: The flowing back of potentially contaminated or polluted water into the potable water supply piping due to a negative pressure in the potable water supply pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A general sanitary condition where wastewater flows back into a fixture or compartment.
flashcards.explanation_long: Backup: A general sanitary condition where wastewater flows back into a fixture or compartment.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A check valve assembly on the sanitary drainage piping that allows effluent to flow in only one direction.
flashcards.explanation_long: Backwater valve: A check valve assembly on the sanitary drainage piping that allows effluent to flow in only one direction.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A tray or partition placed in process equipment or tanks to direct or change the direction of flow.
flashcards.explanation_long: Baffle plate: A tray or partition placed in process equipment or tanks to direct or change the direction of flow.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A check valve that uses a spherical or ball-shaped closure member allowing flow in only one direction.
flashcards.explanation_long: Ball check valve: A check valve that uses a spherical or ball-shaped closure member allowing flow in only one direction.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Ball check valve

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A connection utilizing a ball in a cuplike shell that allows movement in any direction other than along the axis of the pipes.
flashcards.explanation_long: Ball joint: A connection utilizing a ball in a cuplike shell that allows movement in any direction other than along the axis of the pipes.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A type of quarter-turn valve that uses a spherical closure member.
flashcards.explanation_long: Ball valve: A type of quarter-turn valve that uses a spherical closure member.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A condition where no obstruction exists to prevent access by physically challenged individuals.
flashcards.explanation_long: Barrier free: A condition where no obstruction exists to prevent access by physically challenged individuals.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: 1. The lowest point of a stack of vertical pipe. 2. A liquid with a pH of 7.0 or higher.
flashcards.explanation_long: Base: 1. The lowest point of a stack of vertical pipe. 2. A liquid with a pH of 7.0 or higher.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Several plumbing fixtures located together on the same level, generally a water closet, lavatory, and shower or bathtub.
flashcards.explanation_long: Bathroom group: Several plumbing fixtures located together on the same level, generally a water closet, lavatory, and shower or bathtub.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any group of two or more adjacent fixtures.
flashcards.explanation_long: Battery of fixtures: Any group of two or more adjacent fixtures.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Battery of fixtures

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any material in direct contact with a pipe that is under and up to its centerline.
flashcards.explanation_long: Bedding: Any material in direct contact with a pipe that is under and up to its centerline.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Commonly called a hub, that portion of a pipe that is sufficiently enlarged to receive the mating spigot end of another pipe for the purpose of making a joint.
flashcards.explanation_long: Bell: Commonly called a hub, that portion of a pipe that is sufficiently enlarged to receive the mating spigot end of another pipe for the purpose of making a joint.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Commonly called a hub-and-spigot joint for cast-iron soil pipe, a joint in which the spigot is inserted into the hub and the joint is then made tight by caulking or by a gasket.
flashcards.explanation_long: Bell-and-spigot joint: Commonly called a hub-and-spigot joint for cast-iron soil pipe, a joint in which the spigot is inserted into the hub and the joint is then made tight by caulking or by a gasket.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Steel pipe that has not been galvanized.
flashcards.explanation_long: Black pipe: Steel pipe that has not been galvanized.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A solid plate flange used to seal off the end of a pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Blank flange: A solid plate flange used to seal off the end of a pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The temperature of a liquid where the internal vapor pressure is equal to the pressure on the surface of the liquid.
flashcards.explanation_long: Boiling point: The temperature of a liquid where the internal vapor pressure is equal to the pressure on the surface of the liquid.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An outlet on a boiler to permit emptying or discharge of sediment.
flashcards.explanation_long: Boiler blowoff: An outlet on a boiler to permit emptying or discharge of sediment.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: That part of a valve connecting the valve actuator to the valve body; in some valves, it may also contain the stem packing.
flashcards.explanation_long: Bonnet: That part of a valve connecting the valve actuator to the valve body; in some valves, it may also contain the stem packing.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A secondary water heating system used to heat water to a higher temperature than that of the primary heater.
flashcards.explanation_long: Booster water heater: A secondary water heating system used to heat water to a higher temperature than that of the primary heater.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Booster water heater

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any part of a piping system other than a main, riser, or stack.
flashcards.explanation_long: Branch: Any part of a piping system other than a main, riser, or stack.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A length of soil or waste stack corresponding, in general, to a story height, but in no case less than 8 ft (2.4 m) within which the horizontal branches from one floor or story of a building are connected to the soil stack.
flashcards.explanation_long: Branch interval: A length of soil or waste stack corresponding, in general, to a story height, but in no case less than 8 ft (2.4 m) within which the horizontal branches from one floor or story of a building are connected to the soil stack.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A tee having one side branch.
flashcards.explanation_long: Branch tee: A tee having one side branch.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vent connecting one or more individual vents to a vent stack or stack vent.
flashcards.explanation_long: Branch vent: A vent connecting one or more individual vents to a vent stack or stack vent.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The joining of two pipes using a filler metal that has a melting point more than 1,000°F (538 °C).
flashcards.explanation_long: Brazing: The joining of two pipes using a filler metal that has a melting point more than 1,000°F (538 °C).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The ends of a pipe, valve, or fitting that are prepared for brazing.
flashcards.explanation_long: Brazing ends: The ends of a pipe, valve, or fitting that are prepared for brazing.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The amount of heat required to raise the temperature of 1 pound (0.45 kg) of water 1°F (0.565°C).
flashcards.explanation_long: British thermal unit (Btu): The amount of heat required to raise the temperature of 1 pound (0.45 kg) of water 1°F (0.565°C).
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | British thermal unit (Btu)

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Where internal water contact parts of valves known as trim materials (stem, disc, seat rings, etc.) are made of bronze.
flashcards.explanation_long: Bronze-mounted: Where internal water contact parts of valves known as trim materials (stem, disc, seat rings, etc.) are made of bronze.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Abbreviation for British thermal units per hour.
flashcards.explanation_long: Btuh: Abbreviation for British thermal units per hour.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A valve seat that does not allow visible bubbles to appear when the valve is closed.
flashcards.explanation_long: Bubble tight: A valve seat that does not allow visible bubbles to appear when the valve is closed.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A structure having walls and a roof designed and used for housing, shelter, enclosure, or support of people, animals, and property.
flashcards.explanation_long: Building: A structure having walls and a roof designed and used for housing, shelter, enclosure, or support of people, animals, and property.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Often called a house drain, the lowest piping of a drainage system that receives the discharge from soil, waste, and other drainage pipes inside the walls of a building and conveys it outside the building to a distance from 2 to 5 ft (0.70 to 1.70 m). This drain could be sanitary only, stormwater only, or combined stormwater and sanitary.
flashcards.explanation_long: Building drain: Often called a house drain, the lowest piping of a drainage system that receives the discharge from soil, waste, and other drainage pipes inside the walls of a building and conveys it outside the building to a distance from 2 to 5 ft (0.70 to 1.70 m). This drain could be sanitary only, stormwater only, or combined stormwater and sanitary.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Also called a house sewer, that part of the horizontal piping of a drainage system that extends from the end of the building drain outside the building and conveys the wastewater to a public sewer, private sewer, individual sewage-disposal system, or other approved point of disposal.
flashcards.explanation_long: Building sewer: Also called a house sewer, that part of the horizontal piping of a drainage system that extends from the end of the building drain outside the building and conveys the wastewater to a public sewer, private sewer, individual sewage-disposal system, or other approved point of disposal.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: That portion of a drainage system below the building sewer that cannot drain by gravity.
flashcards.explanation_long: Building subdrain: That portion of a drainage system below the building sewer that cannot drain by gravity.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Commonly called a house trap, a device, fitting, or assembly of fittings installed in the house drain to prevent the circulation of air between the house drain and the house sewer. It is usually installed as a running trap.
flashcards.explanation_long: Building trap: Commonly called a house trap, a device, fitting, or assembly of fittings installed in the house drain to prevent the circulation of air between the house drain and the house sewer. It is usually installed as a running trap.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A tee in which the branch is larger than the run.
flashcards.explanation_long: Bull head tee: A tee in which the branch is larger than the run.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Bull head tee

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A roughness of extra metal protruding from a pipe, usually caused by pipe cutting.
flashcards.explanation_long: Burr: A roughness of extra metal protruding from a pipe, usually caused by pipe cutting.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The maximum design pressure, usually four times normal operating pressure, applied to a piping assembly without causing rupture.
flashcards.explanation_long: Burst pressure: The maximum design pressure, usually four times normal operating pressure, applied to a piping assembly without causing rupture.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe fitting that is solid plate with a hole in the center.
flashcards.explanation_long: Bushing: A pipe fitting that is solid plate with a hole in the center.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A valve deriving its name from the wing-like action of the disc, which operates at right angles to the flow.
flashcards.explanation_long: Butterfly valve: A valve deriving its name from the wing-like action of the disc, which operates at right angles to the flow.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A joint made with the two pipes ends or edges brought together and melted at the junction.
flashcards.explanation_long: Butt weld: A joint made with the two pipes ends or edges brought together and melted at the junction.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipeline with valves intended for diverting flow in a different direction or around a piece of equipment.
flashcards.explanation_long: Bypass: A pipeline with valves intended for diverting flow in a different direction or around a piece of equipment.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device used to divert the flow past the part of the system through which it normally passes.
flashcards.explanation_long: Bypass valve: A device used to divert the flow past the part of the system through which it normally passes.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Comparison of the graduation of an instrument with a standard of known accuracy to eliminate variation.
flashcards.explanation_long: Calibration: Comparison of the graduation of an instrument with a standard of known accuracy to eliminate variation.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A small roof protecting a window or entrance.
flashcards.explanation_long: Canopy: A small roof protecting a window or entrance.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: 1. The maximum or minimum flow obtainable under given conditions of media, temperature, pressure, velocity, etc. 2. The volume of media that may be stored in a container or receptacle.
flashcards.explanation_long: Capacity: 1. The maximum or minimum flow obtainable under given conditions of media, temperature, pressure, velocity, etc. 2. The volume of media that may be stored in a container or receptacle.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The action by which a liquid is drawn into a void, primarily observed during soldering.
flashcards.explanation_long: Capillary: The action by which a liquid is drawn into a void, primarily observed during soldering.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A drainage structure used to collect stormwater only to enter a drainage system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Catch basin: A drainage structure used to collect stormwater only to enter a drainage system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The control of electrolytic corrosion by the application of an electric current in such a way that the structure is made to act as the cathode instead of the anode of an electrolytic cell.
flashcards.explanation_long: Cathodic protection: The control of electrolytic corrosion by the application of an electric current in such a way that the structure is made to act as the cathode instead of the anode of an electrolytic cell.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A method of sealing a space tight against the passage of water or gas by means of applying a substance to adjacent surfaces. 26 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
flashcards.explanation_long: Caulking: A method of sealing a space tight against the passage of water or gas by means of applying a substance to adjacent surfaces. 26 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The formation of bubbles in a liquid because of a partial vacuum, which damages adjacent parts when these bubbles revert back to a liquid.
flashcards.explanation_long: Cavitation: The formation of bubbles in a liquid because of a partial vacuum, which damages adjacent parts when these bubbles revert back to a liquid.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The union of two fittings by the insertion of material, accomplished mechanically or chemically.
flashcards.explanation_long: Cement joint: The union of two fittings by the insertion of material, accomplished mechanically or chemically.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Sometimes called a leaching cesspool, an excavation in the ground that receives the discharge of a drainage system and retains the organic matter and solids discharged but permit the liquids to seep through the bottom and sides.
flashcards.explanation_long: Cesspool: Sometimes called a leaching cesspool, an excavation in the ground that receives the discharge of a drainage system and retains the organic matter and solids discharged but permit the liquids to seep through the bottom and sides.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A method of operating a valve by means of a chain-driven wheel.
flashcards.explanation_long: Chainwheel: A method of operating a valve by means of a chain-driven wheel.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A trough through which any media may flow.
flashcards.explanation_long: Channel: A trough through which any media may flow.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A recess in a wall or a space in which pipes can be run.
flashcards.explanation_long: Chase: A recess in a wall or a space in which pipes can be run.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device designed to allow a fluid to pass through in one direction only.
flashcards.explanation_long: Check valve: A device designed to allow a fluid to pass through in one direction only.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Piping that conveys corrosive or harmful industrial, chemical, or processed wastes to a separate drainage system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Chemical waste system: Piping that conveys corrosive or harmful industrial, chemical, or processed wastes to a separate drainage system.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Chemical waste system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The directed route taken by a flow of media from one point to another.
flashcards.explanation_long: Circuit: The directed route taken by a flow of media from one point to another.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A branch vent that serves two or more traps and extends from in front of the last fixture to the con nection with the vent stack.
flashcards.explanation_long: Circuit vent: A branch vent that serves two or more traps and extends from in front of the last fixture to the con nection with the vent stack.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A covered tank used for storing water, placed underground in many cases.
flashcards.explanation_long: Cistern: A covered tank used for storing water, placed underground in many cases.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The potable water supply provided by a public utility.
flashcards.explanation_long: City water: The potable water supply provided by a public utility.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An opening or fitting in a pipe that can be removed for the purpose of cleaning or examining the in terior of the pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Cleanout: An opening or fitting in a pipe that can be removed for the purpose of cleaning or examining the in terior of the pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A particle-controlled area in which filtered air is supplied to maintain a specified level of cleanliness.
flashcards.explanation_long: Clean room: A particle-controlled area in which filtered air is supplied to maintain a specified level of cleanliness.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Clear wastewater drainage from equipment, rooms, and other areas that does not contain contaminants considered harmful.
flashcards.explanation_long: Clear-water waste: Clear wastewater drainage from equipment, rooms, and other areas that does not contain contaminants considered harmful.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A short piece of pipe used to connect various fittings.
flashcards.explanation_long: Close nipple: A short piece of pipe used to connect various fittings.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The impingement of small-diameter aerosols that causes them to merge.
flashcards.explanation_long: Coalescing: The impingement of small-diameter aerosols that causes them to merge.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Often used nomenclature for a faucet.
flashcards.explanation_long: Cock: Often used nomenclature for a faucet.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Regulations, subsequent amendments thereto, and any emergency rules that the department having jurisdiction may lawfully adopt.
flashcards.explanation_long: Code: Regulations, subsequent amendments thereto, and any emergency rules that the department having jurisdiction may lawfully adopt.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The numerical value that describes the increase in unit length and area of volume as a result of heat.
flashcards.explanation_long: Coefficient of expansion: The numerical value that describes the increase in unit length and area of volume as a result of heat.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Coefficient of expansion

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The deformation of a material attributed to the pressure or forces acting at ambient temperatures.
flashcards.explanation_long: Cold flow: The deformation of a material attributed to the pressure or forces acting at ambient temperatures.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Organisms considered to be in the coli aerogenes group.
flashcards.explanation_long: Coliform: Organisms considered to be in the coli aerogenes group.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A fixture that combines one sink and tray or a two or three-compartment sink and/or tray in one unit.
flashcards.explanation_long: Combination fixture: A fixture that combines one sink and tray or a two or three-compartment sink and/or tray in one unit.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A specially designed system of waste piping, embodying the horizontal wet venting of one or more sinks, floor sinks, or floor drains by means of a common waste and vent pipe, adequately sized to provide the free movement of air above the flow line of the drain.
flashcards.explanation_long: Combined waste and vent system: A specially designed system of waste piping, embodying the horizontal wet venting of one or more sinks, floor sinks, or floor drains by means of a common waste and vent pipe, adequately sized to provide the free movement of air above the flow line of the drain.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Combined waste and vent system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any liquid with a closed-cup flash point at or above 37.8°C (100°F), classified as follows: Class II liquid: a flash point at or above 37.8°C (100°F) and below 60°C (140°F); Class IIIA liquid: a flash point at or above 60°C (140°F) and below 93°C (200°F); Class IIIB liquid: a flash point at or above 93°C (200°F).
flashcards.explanation_long: Combustible liquid: Any liquid with a closed-cup flash point at or above 37.8°C (100°F), classified as follows: Class II liquid: a flash point at or above 37.8°C (100°F) and below 60°C (140°F); Class IIIA liquid: a flash point at or above 60°C (140°F) and below 93°C (200°F); Class IIIB liquid: a flash point at or above 93°C (200°F).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The rated percentage of heat produced compared to the actual heat transferred to the medium being heated.
flashcards.explanation_long: Combustion efficiency: The rated percentage of heat produced compared to the actual heat transferred to the medium being heated.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vent that connects at the junction of two fixture drains and serves as a vent for both fixtures.
flashcards.explanation_long: Common vent: A vent that connects at the junction of two fixture drains and serves as a vent for both fixtures.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A mechanical device used to increase the pressure of air or gas.
flashcards.explanation_long: Compressor: A mechanical device used to increase the pressure of air or gas.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Molecules that separate from a gas upon cooling.
flashcards.explanation_long: Condensate: Molecules that separate from a gas upon cooling.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The ability of a substance to conduct heat or electricity.
flashcards.explanation_long: Conductivity: The ability of a substance to conduct heat or electricity.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The piping conveying stormwater from a building to a point of disposal.
flashcards.explanation_long: Conductor: The piping conveying stormwater from a building to a point of disposal.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe or channel for conveying media.
flashcards.explanation_long: Conduit: A pipe or channel for conveying media.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The total number of fixtures, equipment, or devices attached to a system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Connected load: The total number of fixtures, equipment, or devices attached to a system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vent serving more than one fixture vent or stack vent.
flashcards.explanation_long: Confluent vent: A vent serving more than one fixture vent or stack vent.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: All drawings, specifications, and other written papers prepared or assembled for the purpose of describing the design, location, and physical characteristics necessary for obtaining permits and building a facility.
flashcards.explanation_long: Construction documents: All drawings, specifications, and other written papers prepared or assembled for the purpose of describing the design, location, and physical characteristics necessary for obtaining permits and building a facility.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A degradation of quality in any material that creates a hazard to public health.
flashcards.explanation_long: Contamination: A degradation of quality in any material that creates a hazard to public health.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A medium or condition that spoils the nature or quality of another medium.
flashcards.explanation_long: Contaminator: A medium or condition that spoils the nature or quality of another medium.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vertical vent that is a continuation of the drain to which it connects.
flashcards.explanation_long: Continuous vent: A vertical vent that is a continuation of the drain to which it connects.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A drain from two or three fixtures connected to a single trap.
flashcards.explanation_long: Continuous waste: A drain from two or three fixtures connected to a single trap.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A manual or automatic device that regulates a machine or process.
flashcards.explanation_long: Control: A manual or automatic device that regulates a machine or process.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A cabinet containing the motor starter, circuit breaker, disconnect switch, and other control devices for the control of electric motors and internal combustion-engine-driven fire pumps.
flashcards.explanation_long: Controller: A cabinet containing the motor starter, circuit breaker, disconnect switch, and other control devices for the control of electric motors and internal combustion-engine-driven fire pumps.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A stopcock screwed into the street water main to supply the house service connection.
flashcards.explanation_long: Corporation cock: A stopcock screwed into the street water main to supply the house service connection.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The ability of a chemical compound or material to attack, eat away, and damage materials or human beings.
flashcards.explanation_long: Corrosive: The ability of a chemical compound or material to attack, eat away, and damage materials or human beings.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe fitting with female threads used only to connect two pipes in a straight line.
flashcards.explanation_long: Coupling: A pipe fitting with female threads used only to connect two pipes in a straight line.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The elongation of a material due to heat or stress.
flashcards.explanation_long: Creep: The elongation of a material due to heat or stress.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A reference point on a backflow prevention device or vacuum breaker that determines the minimum elevation above the flood level rim of a fixture or receptacle at which the device may be installed. When a backflow prevention device does not bear critical-level marking, the bottom of the vacuum breaker or combination valve or the bottom of any such approved device shall constitute the critical level.
flashcards.explanation_long: Critical level: A reference point on a backflow prevention device or vacuum breaker that determines the minimum elevation above the flood level rim of a fixture or receptacle at which the device may be installed. When a backflow prevention device does not bear critical-level marking, the bottom of the vacuum breaker or combination valve or the bottom of any such approved device shall constitute the critical level.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The transition point at which a liquid and gas state merge into one another.
flashcards.explanation_long: Critical point: The transition point at which a liquid and gas state merge into one another.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe fitting with four branches each at 90 degrees to each other.
flashcards.explanation_long: Cross: A pipe fitting with four branches each at 90 degrees to each other.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any physical connection or arrangement between two otherwise separated piping systems-one of which contains potable water and the other of which contains a liquid or another substance of unknown or questionable safety-whereby flow may occur from one system to the other, the direction of flow depending on the pressure differential between the two systems.
flashcards.explanation_long: Cross-connection: Any physical connection or arrangement between two otherwise separated piping systems-one of which contains potable water and the other of which contains a liquid or another substance of unknown or questionable safety-whereby flow may occur from one system to the other, the direction of flow depending on the pressure differential between the two systems.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe fitting with a double offset, or shaped like the letter U with the ends turned out, used to pass the flow of one pipe past another when the pipes are in the same plane.
flashcards.explanation_long: Crossover: A pipe fitting with a double offset, or shaped like the letter U with the ends turned out, used to pass the flow of one pipe past another when the pipes are in the same plane.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A valve fitted on a transverse pipe so as to open communication between two parallel pipes.
flashcards.explanation_long: Cross valve: A valve fitted on a transverse pipe so as to open communication between two parallel pipes.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The upper part of a trap where the direction of flow is changed from upward to horizontal.
flashcards.explanation_long: Crown: The upper part of a trap where the direction of flow is changed from upward to horizontal.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vent pipe connected at the topmost point of a trap.
flashcards.explanation_long: Crown vent: A vent pipe connected at the topmost point of a trap.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The field of low temperature.
flashcards.explanation_long: Cryogenic: The field of low temperature.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An enclosure or chamber located at the curb that contains a shutoff valve on the supply line for gas or water to a building.
flashcards.explanation_long: Curb box: An enclosure or chamber located at the curb that contains a shutoff valve on the supply line for gas or water to a building.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A drainage structure that allows stormwater to enter a drainage system from an opening in a road.
flashcards.explanation_long: Curb inlet: A drainage structure that allows stormwater to enter a drainage system from an opening in a road.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A valve in a public location that controls the supply of water to a building.
flashcards.explanation_long: Curb valve: A valve in a public location that controls the supply of water to a building.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: 1. To check or reduce. 2. To deaden vibration.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dampen: 1. To check or reduce. 2. To deaden vibration.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe, 2 ft (0.70 m) or more, terminated by means of a plug, cap, or other fitting that closes off the end of a pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dead end: A pipe, 2 ft (0.70 m) or more, terminated by means of a plug, cap, or other fitting that closes off the end of a pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A trap with a longer water seal than that required by code.
flashcards.explanation_long: Deep seal trap: A trap with a longer water seal than that required by code.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Deep seal trap

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A material that changes state in the presence of water.
flashcards.explanation_long: Deliquescent: A material that changes state in the presence of water.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Estimated flow or use expected under specific operating conditions.
flashcards.explanation_long: Demand: Estimated flow or use expected under specific operating conditions.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The ratio of the weight of a substance to its volume.
flashcards.explanation_long: Density: The ratio of the weight of a substance to its volume.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A material that easily adsorbs water vapor. 28 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
flashcards.explanation_long: Desiccant: A material that easily adsorbs water vapor. 28 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The specific point in a piping network where a pipe size is calculated.
flashcards.explanation_long: Design point: The specific point in a piping network where a pipe size is calculated.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A listed device for sensing the visible or invisible products of combustion.
flashcards.explanation_long: Detector, smoke: A listed device for sensing the visible or invisible products of combustion.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The length along the centerline of pipe and fittings.
flashcards.explanation_long: Developed length: The length along the centerline of pipe and fittings.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The temperature at which water in the air will start to condense on a surface.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dewpoint: The temperature at which water in the air will start to condense on a surface.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The nominal inside diameter of a pipe as commercially designated unless otherwise noted.
flashcards.explanation_long: Diameter: The nominal inside diameter of a pipe as commercially designated unless otherwise noted.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A flexible disc that is used as a closure member in some valves.
flashcards.explanation_long: Diaphragm: A flexible disc that is used as a closure member in some valves.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A fitting having insulating parts or material that prohibits the flow of electric current.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dielectric fitting: A fitting having insulating parts or material that prohibits the flow of electric current.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The variance between two target values.
flashcards.explanation_long: Differential: The variance between two target values.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A separation of compounds dissolved in water into ions.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dissociation: A separation of compounds dissolved in water into ions.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A percent of estimated usage compared to the connected load.
flashcards.explanation_long: Diversity factor: A percent of estimated usage compared to the connected load.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A process in which biochemical decomposition of organic matter takes place, resulting in the formation of simple organic and mineral substances.
flashcards.explanation_long: Digestion: A process in which biochemical decomposition of organic matter takes place, resulting in the formation of simple organic and mineral substances.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A closure member in some types of valves that closes off the flow.
flashcards.explanation_long: Disc: A closure member in some types of valves that closes off the flow.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An appliance for washing dishes, glassware, flatware, and utensils.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dishwasher: An appliance for washing dishes, glassware, flatware, and utensils.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The volume or weight of a fluid, displaced by a floating body.
flashcards.explanation_long: Displacement: The volume or weight of a fluid, displaced by a floating body.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A motor-driven appliance that reduces food and other waste by grinding so it can flow through the drainage system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Disposal: A motor-driven appliance that reduces food and other waste by grinding so it can flow through the drainage system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Gases that form ion components between molecules of a fluid or other substance.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dissolved gases: Gases that form ion components between molecules of a fluid or other substance.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A usage percent applied to the water flow rate that lowers the connected load, used because not all fixtures will discharge simultaneously.
flashcards.explanation_long: Diversity factor: A usage percent applied to the water flow rate that lowers the connected load, used because not all fixtures will discharge simultaneously.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Liquid and waterborne wastes derived from ordinary living processes that are free of industrial wastes and of such a character as to permit satisfactory disposal without special treatment into the public sewer or by means of a private sewage disposal system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Domestic sewage: Liquid and waterborne wastes derived from ordinary living processes that are free of industrial wastes and of such a character as to permit satisfactory disposal without special treatment into the public sewer or by means of a private sewage disposal system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Water primarily indented for direct human use, such as that supplied to plumbing fixtures.
flashcards.explanation_long: Domestic water: Water primarily indented for direct human use, such as that supplied to plumbing fixtures.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A watertight tank in a septic system placed between the septic tank and the distribution box and equipped with a pump or automatic siphon designed to discharge sewage intermittently to a disposal field so that rest periods may be provided between discharges.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dosing tank: A watertight tank in a septic system placed between the septic tank and the distribution box and equipped with a pump or automatic siphon designed to discharge sewage intermittently to a disposal field so that rest periods may be provided between discharges.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: When two wedges acting as a closure member in a gate valve are in contact with the seating faces.
flashcards.explanation_long: Double disc: When two wedges acting as a closure member in a gate valve are in contact with the seating faces.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Two changes of direction installed in succession, or series, in continuous pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Double offset: Two changes of direction installed in succession, or series, in continuous pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A valve having two ports to overcome line pressure imbalance.
flashcards.explanation_long: Double-ported valve: A valve having two ports to overcome line pressure imbalance.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A tee made with easy (long-radius) curves between the body and branch.
flashcards.explanation_long: Double-sweep tee: A tee made with easy (long-radius) curves between the body and branch.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any piping running to a lower level.
flashcards.explanation_long: Down: Any piping running to a lower level.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe that carries rainwater from a roof to its ultimate point of disposal.
flashcards.explanation_long: Downspout: A pipe that carries rainwater from a roof to its ultimate point of disposal.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A location in the direction of flow from a referenced point.
flashcards.explanation_long: Downstream: A location in the direction of flow from a referenced point.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any pipe that carries wastewater or waterborne wastes in a building drainage system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Drain: Any pipe that carries wastewater or waterborne wastes in a building drainage system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The area containing a piping arrangement from a septic tank for the purpose of disposing unwanted liquid waste into the soil.
flashcards.explanation_long: Drain field: The area containing a piping arrangement from a septic tank for the purpose of disposing unwanted liquid waste into the soil.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A type of fitting used in drainage systems with a wide radius that allows a smooth flow of waste water with minimum obstructions.
flashcards.explanation_long: Drainage fitting: A type of fitting used in drainage systems with a wide radius that allows a smooth flow of waste water with minimum obstructions.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The piping within a public or private premises that conveys sewage, rainwater, or other liquid wastes to an approved point of disposal immediately outside the building.
flashcards.explanation_long: Drainage system: The piping within a public or private premises that conveys sewage, rainwater, or other liquid wastes to an approved point of disposal immediately outside the building.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A measure of the probable discharge of a fixture or device into a drainage system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Drainage fixture unit (dfu): A measure of the probable discharge of a fixture or device into a drainage system.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Drainage fixture unit (dfu)

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The deviation between actual values over time and a predetermined value.
flashcards.explanation_long: Drift: The deviation between actual values over time and a predetermined value.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The amount a pressure, temperature, liquid level, or differential pressure deviates lower than a set value.
flashcards.explanation_long: Droop: The amount a pressure, temperature, liquid level, or differential pressure deviates lower than a set value.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Piping running to a lower elevation within the same floor level.
flashcards.explanation_long: Drop: Piping running to a lower elevation within the same floor level.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A small elbow having wings cast on each side, which have countersunk holes so they may be fastened by wood screws to a ceiling, wall, or framing timbers.
flashcards.explanation_long: Drop elbow: A small elbow having wings cast on each side, which have countersunk holes so they may be fastened by wood screws to a ceiling, wall, or framing timbers.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A drainage structure installed at the junction of two sewers when one is 2 ft (0.66 m) above the other.
flashcards.explanation_long: Drop manhole: A drainage structure installed at the junction of two sewers when one is 2 ft (0.66 m) above the other.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A tee having wings of the same type as the drop elbow.
flashcards.explanation_long: Drop tee: A tee having wings of the same type as the drop elbow.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The temperature of air as measured by an ordinary thermometer.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dry-bulb temperature: The temperature of air as measured by an ordinary thermometer.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A valve used with a dry-pipe fire protection sprinkler system that separates water and air. When a sprinkler head fuses, this valve opens, allowing water to flow to the sprinkler head.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dry-pipe valve: A valve used with a dry-pipe fire protection sprinkler system that separates water and air. When a sprinkler head fuses, this valve opens, allowing water to flow to the sprinkler head.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Drainage collected during periods of no rain that contains little or no groundwater by infiltra tion and no stormwater at the time of collection.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dry-weather flow: Drainage collected during periods of no rain that contains little or no groundwater by infiltra tion and no stormwater at the time of collection.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pit belowground having porous walls that allow liquid contents to seep into surrounding earth.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dry well: A pit belowground having porous walls that allow liquid contents to seep into surrounding earth.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device that is capable of using more than one heating medium to supply heat.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dual fuel: A device that is capable of using more than one heating medium to supply heat.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: See common vent .
flashcards.explanation_long: Dual vent: See common vent .

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The concentration of a rainstorm, used in the design of a stormwater drainage system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Duration: The concentration of a rainstorm, used in the design of a stormwater drainage system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A soil or waste system in which all piping is of threaded pipe, tubing, or other material of rigid construction, and recessed draining fittings corresponding to the type of piping are used.
flashcards.explanation_long: Durham system: A soil or waste system in which all piping is of threaded pipe, tubing, or other material of rigid construction, and recessed draining fittings corresponding to the type of piping are used.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Brand name for a high-silicon alloy that is resistant to practically all corrosive wastes, with a silicon content of approximately 14.5 percent and acid resistant throughout the entire thickness of the metal.
flashcards.explanation_long: Durion: Brand name for a high-silicon alloy that is resistant to practically all corrosive wastes, with a silicon content of approximately 14.5 percent and acid resistant throughout the entire thickness of the metal.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The length of time a particular device is in operation.
flashcards.explanation_long: Duty cycle: The length of time a particular device is in operation.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A habitable unit with a potable water supply and integral or closely adjacent toilet facilities, intended for people and used for living. DWV Abbreviation for drain, waste, and vent.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dwelling: A habitable unit with a potable water supply and integral or closely adjacent toilet facilities, intended for people and used for living. DWV Abbreviation for drain, waste, and vent.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The vertical weight of earth or backfill over a buried pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Earth load: The vertical weight of earth or backfill over a buried pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Fittings where the openings on either end are offset.
flashcards.explanation_long: Eccentric fittings: Fittings where the openings on either end are offset.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The minimum cross-sectional area at the point of water-supply discharge, measured or expressed in terms of the diameter of a circle or, if the opening is not circular, the diameter of a circle of equivalent cross sectional area, applicable to an air gap.
flashcards.explanation_long: Effective opening: The minimum cross-sectional area at the point of water-supply discharge, measured or expressed in terms of the diameter of a circle or, if the opening is not circular, the diameter of a circle of equivalent cross sectional area, applicable to an air gap.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any substance entering or carried in a drainage system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Effluent: Any substance entering or carried in a drainage system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A tank or pit located below the normal grade of a gravity system that receives sanitary waste and must be emptied by mechanical means.
flashcards.explanation_long: Ejector pit: A tank or pit located below the normal grade of a gravity system that receives sanitary waste and must be emptied by mechanical means.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A mechanical device for removing sanitary waste containing solids from an ejector pit.
flashcards.explanation_long: Ejector pump: A mechanical device for removing sanitary waste containing solids from an ejector pit.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The greatest stress that a material can withstand without permanent deformation after the release of the stress.
flashcards.explanation_long: Elastic limit: The greatest stress that a material can withstand without permanent deformation after the release of the stress.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A rubber-like substance that when stretched to at least two times its length will return to its original shape upon release.
flashcards.explanation_long: Elastomer: A rubber-like substance that when stretched to at least two times its length will return to its original shape upon release.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A fitting that makes a 90-degree angle (unless otherwise specified) between adjacent pipes.
flashcards.explanation_long: Elbow: A fitting that makes a 90-degree angle (unless otherwise specified) between adjacent pipes.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The process of producing chemical changes by passage of an electric current through an electrolyte (as in a cell), where the ions present carry the current by migrating to the electrodes where they may form new substances (as in the deposition of metals or the liberation of gases).
flashcards.explanation_long: Electrolysis: The process of producing chemical changes by passage of an electric current through an electrolyte (as in a cell), where the ions present carry the current by migrating to the electrodes where they may form new substances (as in the deposition of metals or the liberation of gases).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A dissolved impurity of water.
flashcards.explanation_long: Electrolyte: A dissolved impurity of water.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A process of sludge conditioning in which certain constituents are removed by successive decontamina tions with freshwater or plant effluent, thereby reducing the demand for conditioning chemicals.
flashcards.explanation_long: Elutriation: A process of sludge conditioning in which certain constituents are removed by successive decontamina tions with freshwater or plant effluent, thereby reducing the demand for conditioning chemicals.
