flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A method of connecting the parts of a piping system (e.g., threaded, flanged, butt-weld, socket-weld).
flashcards.explanation_long: End connection: A method of connecting the parts of a piping system (e.g., threaded, flanged, butt-weld, socket-weld).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A plumbing system designed by use of modern engineering design criteria.
flashcards.explanation_long: Engineered plumbing system: A plumbing system designed by use of modern engineering design criteria.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Engineered plumbing system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The measured length of pipe with an additional length to compensate for the friction lost to pipe flow, fittings, and valves. 30 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
flashcards.explanation_long: Equivalent run: The measured length of pipe with an additional length to compensate for the friction lost to pipe flow, fittings, and valves. 30 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The gradual destruction of metal or other material by the abrasive or electromechanical action of liquids, gases, solids, or mixtures of these materials.
flashcards.explanation_long: Erosion: The gradual destruction of metal or other material by the abrasive or electromechanical action of liquids, gases, solids, or mixtures of these materials.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The loss of water from soil by both evaporation and transpiration from the plants growing thereon.
flashcards.explanation_long: Evapotranspiration: The loss of water from soil by both evaporation and transpiration from the plants growing thereon.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A plumbing system regulated by code, or any part thereof, that was installed prior to the effective date of an applicable code.
flashcards.explanation_long: Existing work: A plumbing system regulated by code, or any part thereof, that was installed prior to the effective date of an applicable code.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A liquid leaking out of a sewer.
flashcards.explanation_long: Exfiltration: A liquid leaking out of a sewer.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A joint whose primary purpose is to absorb expansion.
flashcards.explanation_long: Expansion joint: A joint whose primary purpose is to absorb expansion.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A piping arrangement with sufficient length to absorb longitudinal thermal expansion due to heat without undue stress.
flashcards.explanation_long: Expansion loop: A piping arrangement with sufficient length to absorb longitudinal thermal expansion due to heat without undue stress.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Piping material, usually cast iron, that is thicker than standard pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Extra heavy: Piping material, usually cast iron, that is thicker than standard pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A mechanical device used to supply water to a plumbing fixture or shut it off.
flashcards.explanation_long: Faucet: A mechanical device used to supply water to a plumbing fixture or shut it off.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The dimensions from the face of the inlet port to the face of the outlet port of a valve or fitting.
flashcards.explanation_long: Face-to-face dimensions: The dimensions from the face of the inlet port to the face of the outlet port of a valve or fitting.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The internal thread in pipe fittings, valves, etc. for making screwed connections.
flashcards.explanation_long: Female thread: The internal thread in pipe fittings, valves, etc. for making screwed connections.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device through which fluid is passed to separate contaminants from it.
flashcards.explanation_long: Filter: A device through which fluid is passed to separate contaminants from it.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A porous device that performs the process of filtration or filtering.
flashcards.explanation_long: Filter element or media: A porous device that performs the process of filtration or filtering.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Filter element or media

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A functionally related group of devices that, when automatically or manually activated, sound an audible or visual warning either on or off the protected premises, signaling a fire.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fire alarm system: A functionally related group of devices that, when automatically or manually activated, sound an audible or visual warning either on or off the protected premises, signaling a fire.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Fire alarm system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An inlet connection that receives pumped water from fire department equipment to supply a building’s fire sprinkler or standpipe system, most commonly made of a brass body with several 2½-inch hose inlets, a check valve for inlet flow only, an automatic drip connection to prevent leftover water from freezing, and escutcheons with integrated or separate labels indicating what system is being served. FDCs can be exposed (mounted on an exterior building wall), flush-mount (mounted on an interior building connection with inlets on the exterior wall), or freestanding (post-mounted outside the building).
flashcards.explanation_long: Fire department connection: An inlet connection that receives pumped water from fire department equipment to supply a building’s fire sprinkler or standpipe system, most commonly made of a brass body with several 2½-inch hose inlets, a check valve for inlet flow only, an automatic drip connection to prevent leftover water from freezing, and escutcheons with integrated or separate labels indicating what system is being served. FDCs can be exposed (mounted on an exterior building wall), flush-mount (mounted on an interior building connection with inlets on the exterior wall), or freestanding (post-mounted outside the building).
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Fire department connection

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any thing or act that increases, or will cause an increase of, the hazard or menace of fire to a degree greater than what is customarily recognized as normal or that will obstruct, delay, hinder, or interfere with the operations of the fire department or the egress of occupants in the event of a fire.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fire hazard: Any thing or act that increases, or will cause an increase of, the hazard or menace of fire to a degree greater than what is customarily recognized as normal or that will obstruct, delay, hinder, or interfere with the operations of the fire department or the egress of occupants in the event of a fire.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A dedicated piping connection used to supply water for fire department use.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fire hydrant: A dedicated piping connection used to supply water for fire department use.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A system of pipes and equipment used exclusively for extinguishing fires.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fire line: A system of pipes and equipment used exclusively for extinguishing fires.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An approved pump with a driver, controls, and accessories used to supply water for fire protection service.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fire pump: An approved pump with a driver, controls, and accessories used to supply water for fire protection service.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vertical-shaft, turbine-type pump in a can (suction vessel) for installation in a pipeline to raise water pressure.
flashcards.explanation_long: Can pump: A vertical-shaft, turbine-type pump in a can (suction vessel) for installation in a pipeline to raise water pressure.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pump in which the pressure is developed principally by the action of centrifugal force.
flashcards.explanation_long: Centrifugal pump: A pump in which the pressure is developed principally by the action of centrifugal force.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A single-suction pump with its suction nozzle on the opposite side of the casing from the stuffing box and the face of the suction nozzle perpendicular to the longitudinal axis of the shaft.
flashcards.explanation_long: End-suction pump: A single-suction pump with its suction nozzle on the opposite side of the casing from the stuffing box and the face of the suction nozzle perpendicular to the longitudinal axis of the shaft.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A low-flow, high-head pump for sprinkler systems not being supplied from a fire pump that pressurizes the sprinkler system so the loss of water supply pressure will not cause a false alarm.
flashcards.explanation_long: Excess pressure pump: A low-flow, high-head pump for sprinkler systems not being supplied from a fire pump that pressurizes the sprinkler system so the loss of water supply pressure will not cause a false alarm.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Excess pressure pump

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pump with the shaft normally in a horizontal position.
flashcards.explanation_long: Horizontal pump: A pump with the shaft normally in a horizontal position.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A centrifugal pump characterized by a housing that is split parallel to the shaft.
flashcards.explanation_long: Horizontal split-case pump: A centrifugal pump characterized by a housing that is split parallel to the shaft.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Horizontal split-case pump

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A centrifugal pump in which the drive unit is supported by the pump, with its suction and discharge flanges on approximately the same centerline.
flashcards.explanation_long: Inline pump: A centrifugal pump in which the drive unit is supported by the pump, with its suction and discharge flanges on approximately the same centerline.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pump with controls and accessories used to maintain pressure in a fire protection system without the operation of the fire pump.
flashcards.explanation_long: Pressure maintenance (jockey) pump: A pump with controls and accessories used to maintain pressure in a fire protection system without the operation of the fire pump.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Pressure maintenance (jockey) pump

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A centrifugal pump with one or more impellers discharging into one or more bowls and a vertical educator or column pipe used to connect the bowls to the discharge head on which the pump driver is mounted.
flashcards.explanation_long: Vertical shaft turbine pump: A centrifugal pump with one or more impellers discharging into one or more bowls and a vertical educator or column pipe used to connect the bowls to the discharge head on which the pump driver is mounted.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Vertical shaft turbine pump

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device used to connect pipes or change the direction of straight runs of pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fitting: A device used to connect pipes or change the direction of straight runs of pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A fitting designed to join pipe or tubing by means of pressure or friction.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fitting, compression: A fitting designed to join pipe or tubing by means of pressure or friction.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A fitting that utilizes a radially extending collar for sealing and connection.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fitting, flange: A fitting that utilizes a radially extending collar for sealing and connection.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe, not considered a main, connecting several fixtures.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fixture branch: A pipe, not considered a main, connecting several fixtures.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device designed to support a plumbing fixture.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fixture carrier: A device designed to support a plumbing fixture.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The drain from the trap of a fixture to the junction of that drain with any other drain pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fixture drain: The drain from the trap of a fixture to the junction of that drain with any other drain pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A water supply pipe connecting to a fixture from a branch or main.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fixture supply: A water supply pipe connecting to a fixture from a branch or main.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A numeric value representing the probable rate of drainage discharge into a drainage system by various types of plumbing fixtures or equipment.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fixture unit, drainage (dfu): A numeric value representing the probable rate of drainage discharge into a drainage system by various types of plumbing fixtures or equipment.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Fixture unit, drainage (dfu)

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A numeric value representing the probable rate of water supply used by various types of plumbing fixtures or equipment.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fixture unit, water (wfu): A numeric value representing the probable rate of water supply used by various types of plumbing fixtures or equipment.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Fixture unit, water (wfu)

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Capable of being ignited.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flammable: Capable of being ignited.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any substance that exists in the gaseous state at normal atmospheric temperature and pressure and is capable of being ignited and burned when mixed with the proper proportion of air, oxygen, or other oxidizers.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flammable gas: Any substance that exists in the gaseous state at normal atmospheric temperature and pressure and is capable of being ignited and burned when mixed with the proper proportion of air, oxygen, or other oxidizers.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A liquid that has a closed-cup flash point below 37.8°C (100°F) and a maximum vapor pressure of 2,068 mmHg (40 psi absolute) at 37.8°C (100°F).
flashcards.explanation_long: Flammable liquid: A liquid that has a closed-cup flash point below 37.8°C (100°F) and a maximum vapor pressure of 2,068 mmHg (40 psi absolute) at 37.8°C (100°F).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A ring-shaped plate on the end of a pipe at right angles to the end of the pipe and provided with holes for bolts to allow the pipe to be fastened to a similarly equipped adjoining pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flange: A ring-shaped plate on the end of a pipe at right angles to the end of the pipe and provided with holes for bolts to allow the pipe to be fastened to a similarly equipped adjoining pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A valve or fitting having plain-faced, raised-face, large male-and female, large tongue-and-groove, small tongue-and-groove, or ring-joint flanges for joining to other piping elements.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flange end: A valve or fitting having plain-faced, raised-face, large male-and female, large tongue-and-groove, small tongue-and-groove, or ring-joint flanges for joining to other piping elements.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe flange in which the entire surface of the flange is faced straight across and uses either a full face or ring gasket.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flange faces: A pipe flange in which the entire surface of the flange is faced straight across and uses either a full face or ring gasket.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any waterproof material fitted over a surface where water is expected to run.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flashing: Any waterproof material fitted over a surface where water is expected to run.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The temperature at which a fluid gives off flammable vapor in sufficient concentration to form an ignitable mixture.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flash point: The temperature at which a fluid gives off flammable vapor in sufficient concentration to form an ignitable mixture.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A valve that is operated by means of a bulb or ball floating on the surface of a liquid within a tank. The rising and falling action operates a lever, which opens and closes the valve.
flashcards.explanation_long: Float valve: A valve that is operated by means of a bulb or ball floating on the surface of a liquid within a tank. The rising and falling action operates a lever, which opens and closes the valve.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The condition when liquid rises to the flood level rim of a fixture.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flooded: The condition when liquid rises to the flood level rim of a fixture.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The top edge of a receptacle or fixture from which water overflows.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flood level rim: The top edge of a receptacle or fixture from which water overflows.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Flood level rim

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The pressure in a water supply pipe near the water outlet while the faucet or water outlet is fully open and flowing.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flow pressure: The pressure in a water supply pipe near the water outlet while the faucet or water outlet is fully open and flowing.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A buoyant force that causes a buried tank to rise.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flotation: A buoyant force that causes a buried tank to rise.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An enclosed passage, primarily vertical, for removal of the gaseous products of combustion to the exterior.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flue: An enclosed passage, primarily vertical, for removal of the gaseous products of combustion to the exterior.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An atmospheric receptacle holding water integrated with a water closet designed to discharge a pre determined quantity of water to flush a water closet.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flush tank: An atmospheric receptacle holding water integrated with a water closet designed to discharge a pre determined quantity of water to flush a water closet.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pressurized device that supplies a predetermined quantity of water to water closets and other similar fixtures and is closed by direct pressure or other mechanical means. Also called flushometer valve.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flush valve: A pressurized device that supplies a predetermined quantity of water to water closets and other similar fixtures and is closed by direct pressure or other mechanical means. Also called flushometer valve.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A floor drain that is equipped with an integral water supply, enabling flushing of the drain receptor and trap.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flushing-type floor drain: A floor drain that is equipped with an integral water supply, enabling flushing of the drain receptor and trap.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Flushing-type floor drain

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A paste used to aid the flowing characteristics and to prevent oxidation of brazed or soldered joints.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flux: A paste used to aid the flowing characteristics and to prevent oxidation of brazed or soldered joints.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The lowest part of a foundation wall or column resting on the bearing soil, rock, or piling that transmits the superimposed load to the bearing material. 32 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
flashcards.explanation_long: Footing: The lowest part of a foundation wall or column resting on the bearing soil, rock, or piling that transmits the superimposed load to the bearing material. 32 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A special pipe installed at or below a footing to remove accumulated groundwater or rainwater.
flashcards.explanation_long: Footing drain: A special pipe installed at or below a footing to remove accumulated groundwater or rainwater.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A check valve installed at the base of a pump-suction pipe to maintain prime by preventing pumped liquid from draining away from the pump.
flashcards.explanation_long: Foot valve: A check valve installed at the base of a pump-suction pipe to maintain prime by preventing pumped liquid from draining away from the pump.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pumped sewer under pressure.
flashcards.explanation_long: Force main: A pumped sewer under pressure.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A percent reduction used in water heating devices to account for obstructions in the heating coils.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fouling factor: A percent reduction used in water heating devices to account for obstructions in the heating coils.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A drain consisting of an underground passage made by filling a trench with loose stones and covering it with earth. Also called rubble drain.
flashcards.explanation_long: French drain: A drain consisting of an underground passage made by filling a trench with loose stones and covering it with earth. Also called rubble drain.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vent line connected with the building drain upstream of the house trap and extending to the outer air, which provides air circulation between the house drain and the public sewer.
flashcards.explanation_long: Fresh-air inlet: A vent line connected with the building drain upstream of the house trap and extending to the outer air, which provides air circulation between the house drain and the public sewer.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A quantity that relates to the head loss of a fluid’s velocity while the fluid is flowing through a specific length and diameter of pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Friction factor: A quantity that relates to the head loss of a fluid’s velocity while the fluid is flowing through a specific length and diameter of pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device containing water that will not freeze at a low temperature.
flashcards.explanation_long: Frostproof: A device containing water that will not freeze at a low temperature.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An interchange of atoms carrying an electric charge between materials. The anode metal with the higher electrode potential corrodes; the cathode is protected.
flashcards.explanation_long: Galvanic action: An interchange of atoms carrying an electric charge between materials. The anode metal with the higher electrode potential corrodes; the cathode is protected.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A process where the surface of iron or steel piping or plate is covered with a layer of zinc.
flashcards.explanation_long: Galvanizing: A process where the surface of iron or steel piping or plate is covered with a layer of zinc.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A document referred to in criteria or code and accepted by the administrative au thority.
flashcards.explanation_long: Generally accepted standard: A document referred to in criteria or code and accepted by the administrative au thority.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Generally accepted standard

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: 1. The surface of the ground or the slope or fall of a line of pipe in reference to a horizontal plane. 2. In drainage systems, the fall in a fraction of an inch or percentage slope per foot (mm/m) length of pipe. 3. The quality of a material.
flashcards.explanation_long: Grade: 1. The surface of the ground or the slope or fall of a line of pipe in reference to a horizontal plane. 2. In drainage systems, the fall in a fraction of an inch or percentage slope per foot (mm/m) length of pipe. 3. The quality of a material.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device that allows stormwater to enter the top of a drainage structure while preventing the entrance of debris.
flashcards.explanation_long: Grating: A device that allows stormwater to enter the top of a drainage structure while preventing the entrance of debris.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A course material of sizes ¾ inch to 3 inches.
flashcards.explanation_long: Gravel: A course material of sizes ¾ inch to 3 inches.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A plumbing appurtenance that is installed in a sanitary drainage system to intercept oily and greasy wastes from a wastewater discharge and is capable of intercepting free-floating fats and oils.
flashcards.explanation_long: Grease interceptor: A plumbing appurtenance that is installed in a sanitary drainage system to intercept oily and greasy wastes from a wastewater discharge and is capable of intercepting free-floating fats and oils.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Effluent discharge that is produced from food processing, food preparation, or other sources where grease, fats, and oils enter automatic dishwater pre-rinse stations, sinks, or other appurtenances.
flashcards.explanation_long: Grease-laden waste: Effluent discharge that is produced from food processing, food preparation, or other sources where grease, fats, and oils enter automatic dishwater pre-rinse stations, sinks, or other appurtenances.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A solids-handling pump that grinds sewage solids to a fine slurry, rather than passing through entire spherical solids.
flashcards.explanation_long: Grinder pump: A solids-handling pump that grinds sewage solids to a fine slurry, rather than passing through entire spherical solids.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Water found at or below grade extending down to an impervious layer.
flashcards.explanation_long: Groundwater: Water found at or below grade extending down to an impervious layer.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device used to allow axial pipe movement only.
flashcards.explanation_long: Guide (piping): A device used to allow axial pipe movement only.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A document that is advisory or informative in nature and contains only nonmandatory provi sions, thus is not suitable for adoption into law.
flashcards.explanation_long: Guide (document): A document that is advisory or informative in nature and contains only nonmandatory provi sions, thus is not suitable for adoption into law.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An open horizontal channel that carries stormwater away from a roof surface.
flashcards.explanation_long: Gutter: An open horizontal channel that carries stormwater away from a roof surface.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A colorless, odorless, electrically nonconductive gas for extinguishing fires that is no longer used due to environmental considerations.
flashcards.explanation_long: Halon 1301: A colorless, odorless, electrically nonconductive gas for extinguishing fires that is no longer used due to environmental considerations.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device used to suspend pipes or equipment within a building or structure.
flashcards.explanation_long: Hanger: A device used to suspend pipes or equipment within a building or structure.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The portion of a buried pipe below the centerline.
flashcards.explanation_long: Haunch: The portion of a buried pipe below the centerline.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A chemical with one or more of the hazard ratings as defined in NFPA 704: Standard System (For the hazard ratings of many for the Identification of the Hazards of Materials for Emergency Response Health. chemicals, see NFPA 49: Table of Common Hazardous Chemicals and NFPA 325: Guide to Fire Hazard Proper ties of Flammable Liquids, Gases, and Volatile Solids .)
flashcards.explanation_long: Hazardous chemical: A chemical with one or more of the hazard ratings as defined in NFPA 704: Standard System (For the hazard ratings of many for the Identification of the Hazards of Materials for Emergency Response Health. chemicals, see NFPA 49: Table of Common Hazardous Chemicals and NFPA 325: Guide to Fire Hazard Proper ties of Flammable Liquids, Gases, and Volatile Solids .)

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A unit of measure representing the relative energy of a static or flowing fluid.
flashcards.explanation_long: Head: A unit of measure representing the relative energy of a static or flowing fluid.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe that does not diminish in size.
flashcards.explanation_long: Header: A pipe that does not diminish in size.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The energy loss of a fluid as it passes through a flow passage. Also called pressure drop.
flashcards.explanation_long: Head loss: The energy loss of a fluid as it passes through a flow passage. Also called pressure drop.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device specifically designed and constructed to efficiently transfer heat energy from a hot fluid to a cooler fluid.
flashcards.explanation_long: Heat exchanger: A device specifically designed and constructed to efficiently transfer heat energy from a hot fluid to a cooler fluid.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A continuous or intermittent application of heat to a pipe or vessel to replace the heat lost to ambi ent air.
flashcards.explanation_long: Heat tracing: A continuous or intermittent application of heat to a pipe or vessel to replace the heat lost to ambi ent air.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The movement of heat energy.
flashcards.explanation_long: Heat transfer: The movement of heat energy.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe or fitting that makes an angle of less than 45 degrees with the horizontal.
flashcards.explanation_long: Horizontal: A pipe or fitting that makes an angle of less than 45 degrees with the horizontal.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A faucet installed on the outside wall of a building for the supply of potable water.
flashcards.explanation_long: Hose bibb: A faucet installed on the outside wall of a building for the supply of potable water.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Water at a temperature higher than ambient established by generally accepted practice or code as being suitable for a specific application (e.g., water at a temperature greater than or equal to 110°F [43°C] as defined in the International Plumbing Code).
flashcards.explanation_long: Hot water: Water at a temperature higher than ambient established by generally accepted practice or code as being suitable for a specific application (e.g., water at a temperature greater than or equal to 110°F [43°C] as defined in the International Plumbing Code).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A house sewer.
flashcards.explanation_long: House drain: A house sewer.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A building trap.
flashcards.explanation_long: House trap: A building trap.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A joint made with an enlarged diameter or hub at one end and a spigot at the other end, tightened by oakum and lead or by a neoprene gasket caulked or inserted in the hub around the spigot. Generally referred to as a caulked joint.
flashcards.explanation_long: Hub-and-spigot: A joint made with an enlarged diameter or hub at one end and a spigot at the other end, tightened by oakum and lead or by a neoprene gasket caulked or inserted in the hub around the spigot. Generally referred to as a caulked joint.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: 1. A drainage receptacle with a raised rim and without a strainer or grate. 2. A floor drain incapable of receiving surface drainage (commonly used for equipment condensation drainage).
flashcards.explanation_long: Hub drain: 1. A drainage receptacle with a raised rim and without a strainer or grate. 2. A floor drain incapable of receiving surface drainage (commonly used for equipment condensation drainage).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Soil piping with plain ends, with a joint consisting of a stainless steel or cast-iron clamp and neoprene gasket assembly.
flashcards.explanation_long: Hubless: Soil piping with plain ends, with a joint consisting of a stainless steel or cast-iron clamp and neoprene gasket assembly.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The percent of water vapor in the air compared to the saturated amount of water vapor possible at the temperature when measured. Often called relative humidity.
flashcards.explanation_long: Humidity: The percent of water vapor in the air compared to the saturated amount of water vapor possible at the temperature when measured. Often called relative humidity.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A valve or faucet for drawing water from a pipe in large quantities, usually used in reference to a fire department water supply.
flashcards.explanation_long: Hydrant: A valve or faucet for drawing water from a pipe in large quantities, usually used in reference to a fire department water supply.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Furthest from the source of supply in terms of total pressure lost through the entire system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Hydraulically remote: Furthest from the source of supply in terms of total pressure lost through the entire system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A liquid incapable of being dissolved in water.
flashcards.explanation_long: Immiscible: A liquid incapable of being dissolved in water.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A rotating part of a pump that imparts velocity to the liquid by centrifugal force.
flashcards.explanation_long: Impeller: A rotating part of a pump that imparts velocity to the liquid by centrifugal force.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any physical, chemical, or biological substance found in water that makes it unsuitable for the purpose intended or as a source of potable water.
flashcards.explanation_long: Impurity: Any physical, chemical, or biological substance found in water that makes it unsuitable for the purpose intended or as a source of potable water.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A discharge to the drainage system or receptacle through an air gap as a method to avoid cross connections.
flashcards.explanation_long: Indirect waste: A discharge to the drainage system or receptacle through an air gap as a method to avoid cross connections.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe that is installed to vent only one fixture trap and connects with the vent system above the fixture served or terminates in the open air.
flashcards.explanation_long: Individual vent: A pipe that is installed to vent only one fixture trap and connects with the vent system above the fixture served or terminates in the open air.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Loss of liquid from a fixture trap due to the pressure differential between the inlet and outlet of a trap, often caused by the discharge of another fixture.
flashcards.explanation_long: Induced siphonage: Loss of liquid from a fixture trap due to the pressure differential between the inlet and outlet of a trap, often caused by the discharge of another fixture.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: All liquid or waterborne waste from industrial or commercial processes that has properties other than domestic sewage.
flashcards.explanation_long: Industrial waste: All liquid or waterborne waste from industrial or commercial processes that has properties other than domestic sewage.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A gas that does not react with other materials at ordinary temperatures and pressures.
flashcards.explanation_long: Inert: A gas that does not react with other materials at ordinary temperatures and pressures.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Liquid leaking into a sewer.
flashcards.explanation_long: Infiltration: Liquid leaking into a sewer.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Surface water flowing into a catch basin, manhole, or other collection device.
flashcards.explanation_long: Inflow: Surface water flowing into a catch basin, manhole, or other collection device.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device in compressed air service that cleans air entering a compressor.
flashcards.explanation_long: Inlet filter: A device in compressed air service that cleans air entering a compressor.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A chemical substance of mineral origin.
flashcards.explanation_long: Inorganic: A chemical substance of mineral origin.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The amount required for proper operation of any device.
flashcards.explanation_long: Input: The amount required for proper operation of any device.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A water heater designed to heat water only upon demand.
flashcards.explanation_long: Instantaneous water heater: A water heater designed to heat water only upon demand.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Instantaneous water heater

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: 1. An alternative for nitrogen intended for the powering of medical devices unrelated to human respiration (e.g., surgical tools, ceiling arms). It is a medical support gas that falls under the general requirements for medical gases. 2. Air intended for powering industrial equipment and devices, cleaning, service, and maintenance. 34 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
flashcards.explanation_long: Instrument air: 1. An alternative for nitrogen intended for the powering of medical devices unrelated to human respiration (e.g., surgical tools, ceiling arms). It is a medical support gas that falls under the general requirements for medical gases. 2. Air intended for powering industrial equipment and devices, cleaning, service, and maintenance. 34 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device that separates, retains, and allows removal of any specific material suspended in a waste stream, while permitting acceptable liquids to flow freely into a drainage system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Interceptor: A device that separates, retains, and allows removal of any specific material suspended in a waste stream, while permitting acceptable liquids to flow freely into a drainage system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The lowest point on the interior of a horizontal pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Invert: The lowest point on the interior of a horizontal pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An atom or group of atoms that has an electrical charge.
flashcards.explanation_long: Ion: An atom or group of atoms that has an electrical charge.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The compressing of air under constant pressure.
flashcards.explanation_long: Isobaric process: The compressing of air under constant pressure.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The compressing of air under constant volume.
flashcards.explanation_long: Isochoric process: The compressing of air under constant volume.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The compressing of air under constant temperature.
flashcards.explanation_long: Isothermal process: The compressing of air under constant temperature.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Equipment, products, or materials bearing a label of a listing agency.
flashcards.explanation_long: Labeled: Equipment, products, or materials bearing a label of a listing agency.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A small faucet in a bench used for dispensing water or gas in laboratories.
flashcards.explanation_long: Laboratory outlet: A small faucet in a bench used for dispensing water or gas in laboratories.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A drainage pipe that does not receive sewage from any other common sewer except house connections.
flashcards.explanation_long: Lateral sewer: A drainage pipe that does not receive sewage from any other common sewer except house connections.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: See dry well .
flashcards.explanation_long: Leaching well: See dry well .

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Piping containing only stormwater.
flashcards.explanation_long: Leader: Piping containing only stormwater.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The discharge from any fixture, appliance, or appurtenance in connection with a plumbing system that does not receive fecal matter.
flashcards.explanation_long: Liquid waste: The discharge from any fixture, appliance, or appurtenance in connection with a plumbing system that does not receive fecal matter.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Equipment, products, or materials approved by a third-party testing organization and acceptable to the authority having jurisdiction.
flashcards.explanation_long: Listed: Equipment, products, or materials approved by a third-party testing organization and acceptable to the authority having jurisdiction.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An agency accepted by the administrative authority to list or label certain models of a product and maintain a periodic inspection program on the current production of listed models to indicate that the products have been tested, comply with generally accepted standards, and are safe for use in a specified manner.
flashcards.explanation_long: Listing agency: An agency accepted by the administrative authority to list or label certain models of a product and maintain a periodic inspection program on the current production of listed models to indicate that the products have been tested, comply with generally accepted standards, and are safe for use in a specified manner.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The percentage of the total connected fixture unit flow that is likely to occur at any point in a drain age system, representing the ratio of the probable load to the connected load and determined by the average rates of flow of the various kinds of fixtures, average frequency of use, duration of flow during one use, and number of fixtures installed.
flashcards.explanation_long: Load factor: The percentage of the total connected fixture unit flow that is likely to occur at any point in a drain age system, representing the ratio of the probable load to the connected load and determined by the average rates of flow of the various kinds of fixtures, average frequency of use, duration of flow during one use, and number of fixtures installed.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vent serving two or more traps and extending from the front of the last fixture to a stack vent.
flashcards.explanation_long: Loop vent: A vent serving two or more traps and extending from the front of the last fixture to a stack vent.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The principal artery of a system of continuous piping to which branches may be connected.
flashcards.explanation_long: Main: The principal artery of a system of continuous piping to which branches may be connected.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vent header to which vent stacks are connected.
flashcards.explanation_long: Main vent: A vent header to which vent stacks are connected.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Capable of being extended or shaped. Most metals are malleable.
flashcards.explanation_long: Malleable: Capable of being extended or shaped. Most metals are malleable.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A drainage structure used for allowing access into a sewer, allowing water to enter a sewer, or allowing easy connection when placed at the junction of sewers.
flashcards.explanation_long: Manhole: A drainage structure used for allowing access into a sewer, allowing water to enter a sewer, or allowing easy connection when placed at the junction of sewers.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device used to connect the outlets of one or more gas cylinders to the central piping system for that specific gas.
flashcards.explanation_long: Manifold (medical): A device used to connect the outlets of one or more gas cylinders to the central piping system for that specific gas.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A multi-opening header to which one or more branch lines connect.
flashcards.explanation_long: Manifold (plumbing): A multi-opening header to which one or more branch lines connect.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An individual who is licensed and authorized to install and assume responsibility for contractual agreements pertaining to plumbing and to secure any required permits.
flashcards.explanation_long: Master plumber: An individual who is licensed and authorized to install and assume responsibility for contractual agreements pertaining to plumbing and to secure any required permits.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The most connected devices that may be expected to be in use at any one time.
flashcards.explanation_long: Maximum probable demand: The most connected devices that may be expected to be in use at any one time.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Maximum probable demand

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Pharmaceutical air used for life support, supplied from cylinders, bulk containers, or medical air compressors or reconstituted from oxygen USP and oil-free, dry nitrogen NF.
flashcards.explanation_long: Medical air: Pharmaceutical air used for life support, supplied from cylinders, bulk containers, or medical air compressors or reconstituted from oxygen USP and oil-free, dry nitrogen NF.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: One of four distinct types of alarms in Category 1 and Category 2 medical gas sys tems-master alarms, area alarms, local alarms, and Category 3 alarms-as recognized by NFPA 99: Healthcare Facilities Code.
flashcards.explanation_long: Medical gas alarm system: One of four distinct types of alarms in Category 1 and Category 2 medical gas sys tems-master alarms, area alarms, local alarms, and Category 3 alarms-as recognized by NFPA 99: Healthcare Facilities Code.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Medical gas alarm system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A warning system within an area of use that provides continuous visible and audible surveillance of Category 1 and Category 2 medical gas and vacuum systems, typically located in treatment areas at or near the nurses’ station to monitor and report on conditions for the benefit of the staff in that area.
flashcards.explanation_long: Area alarm system: A warning system within an area of use that provides continuous visible and audible surveillance of Category 1 and Category 2 medical gas and vacuum systems, typically located in treatment areas at or near the nurses’ station to monitor and report on conditions for the benefit of the staff in that area.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Area alarm system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A warning system within an area of use that provides continuous visible and audible surveillance of Category 3 medical gas systems, commonly used in a medical gas system with low risk to the patient, such as in a dental office.
flashcards.explanation_long: Category 3 alarm system: A warning system within an area of use that provides continuous visible and audible surveillance of Category 3 medical gas systems, commonly used in a medical gas system with low risk to the patient, such as in a dental office.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Category 3 alarm system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A warning system that provides continuous visible and audible surveillance of medical gas and vacuum system source equipment at the equipment site, placed on or very near the source equipment they monitor.
flashcards.explanation_long: Local alarm system: A warning system that provides continuous visible and audible surveillance of medical gas and vacuum system source equipment at the equipment site, placed on or very near the source equipment they monitor.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Local alarm system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A warning system that monitors the operation and condition of the source of supply, the reserve source (if any), and the pressure in the main lines of each medical gas and vacuum piping system, used to indicate the condition of the sources of supply (which may be remote) at locations that are attended whenever the building is occupied and where the staff who will act to correct any failures will see the alarm.
flashcards.explanation_long: Master alarm system: A warning system that monitors the operation and condition of the source of supply, the reserve source (if any), and the pressure in the main lines of each medical gas and vacuum piping system, used to indicate the condition of the sources of supply (which may be remote) at locations that are attended whenever the building is occupied and where the staff who will act to correct any failures will see the alarm.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Master alarm system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An assembly of equipment and piping used to distribute nonflammable medical gases such as oxygen, nitrous oxide, compressed air, carbon dioxide, and helium for direct patient application from central supply systems, with pressure and operating controls, alarm warning systems, related components, and piping networks extending to station outlet valves at patient use points.
flashcards.explanation_long: Medical gas system: An assembly of equipment and piping used to distribute nonflammable medical gases such as oxygen, nitrous oxide, compressed air, carbon dioxide, and helium for direct patient application from central supply systems, with pressure and operating controls, alarm warning systems, related components, and piping networks extending to station outlet valves at patient use points.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Medical gas system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Nitrogen or instrument air falling under the general requirements for medical gases used for any medical support purpose (e.g., to remove excess moisture from instruments before further processing or to operate medical-surgical tools, air-driven booms, pendants, or similar applications) and not respired as part of any treatment.
flashcards.explanation_long: Medical support gas: Nitrogen or instrument air falling under the general requirements for medical gases used for any medical support purpose (e.g., to remove excess moisture from instruments before further processing or to operate medical-surgical tools, air-driven booms, pendants, or similar applications) and not respired as part of any treatment.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Medical support gas

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A method used to provide a source of drainage, aspiration, and suction to remove body fluids from patients.
flashcards.explanation_long: Medical-surgical vacuum: A method used to provide a source of drainage, aspiration, and suction to remove body fluids from patients.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A system consisting of central vacuum-producing equipment with pressure and operating controls, shutoff valves, alarm warning systems, gauges, and a network of piping extending to and terminating with suitable stations inlets at locations where patient suction may be required.
flashcards.explanation_long: Medical-surgical vacuum system: A system consisting of central vacuum-producing equipment with pressure and operating controls, shutoff valves, alarm warning systems, gauges, and a network of piping extending to and terminating with suitable stations inlets at locations where patient suction may be required.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Medical-surgical vacuum system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An aerosol suspended in air composed of liquid particles.
flashcards.explanation_long: Mist: An aerosol suspended in air composed of liquid particles.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Observation, sampling, or testing at designated locations.
flashcards.explanation_long: Monitoring: Observation, sampling, or testing at designated locations.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Pressure less than atmospheric.
flashcards.explanation_long: Negative pressure: Pressure less than atmospheric.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Water not safe for drinking, personal, or culinary use.
flashcards.explanation_long: Nonpotable water: Water not safe for drinking, personal, or culinary use.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The design or expected force per unit area at any point of a system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Normal pressure: The design or expected force per unit area at any point of a system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The purpose for which a building, structure, or portion thereof is utilized or occupied.
flashcards.explanation_long: Occupancy: The purpose for which a building, structure, or portion thereof is utilized or occupied.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A classification that designates the combustibility of the contents in an area, used for fire suppression system design.
flashcards.explanation_long: Occupancy classification: A classification that designates the combustibility of the contents in an area, used for fire suppression system design.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Occupancies or portions of other occupancies where the quantity and combustibility of contents is very high, and flammable and combustible liquids, dust, lint, or other materials are present, introducing the probability of rapidly developing fires with high rates of heat release.
flashcards.explanation_long: Extra hazard: Occupancies or portions of other occupancies where the quantity and combustibility of contents is very high, and flammable and combustible liquids, dust, lint, or other materials are present, introducing the probability of rapidly developing fires with high rates of heat release.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Occupancies with little or no flammable or combustible liquids.
flashcards.explanation_long: Extra hazard group 1: Occupancies with little or no flammable or combustible liquids.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Extra hazard group 1

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Occupancies with moderate to substantial amounts of flammable or combustible liquids or where shielding of combustibles is extensive.
flashcards.explanation_long: Extra hazard group 2: Occupancies with moderate to substantial amounts of flammable or combustible liquids or where shielding of combustibles is extensive.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Extra hazard group 2

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Occupancies or portions of other occupancies where combustibility is low, the quan tity of combustibles does not exceed 8 feet (2.4 m), and fires with moderate rates of heat release are expected.
flashcards.explanation_long: Ordinary hazard group 1: Occupancies or portions of other occupancies where combustibility is low, the quan tity of combustibles does not exceed 8 feet (2.4 m), and fires with moderate rates of heat release are expected.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Ordinary hazard group 1

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Occupancies or portions of other occupancies where the quantity and combustibility of contents are moderate to high, stockpiles do not exceed 12 feet (3.7 m), and fires with moderate to high rates of heat release are expected.
flashcards.explanation_long: Ordinary hazard group 2: Occupancies or portions of other occupancies where the quantity and combustibility of contents are moderate to high, stockpiles do not exceed 12 feet (3.7 m), and fires with moderate to high rates of heat release are expected.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Ordinary hazard group 2

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Occupancies or portions of other occupancies where the quantity and/or combustibility of contents is low, and fires with relatively low rates of heat release are expected.
flashcards.explanation_long: Light hazard: Occupancies or portions of other occupancies where the quantity and/or combustibility of contents is low, and fires with relatively low rates of heat release are expected.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A combination of fittings that takes a pipe out of line and places it parallel to the original pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Offset: A combination of fittings that takes a pipe out of line and places it parallel to the original pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Free air outside any structure.
flashcards.explanation_long: Open air: Free air outside any structure.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A floor drainage receptacle without a strainer or grate capable of receiving surface drainage, com monly used in mechanical rooms for equipment pressure and temperature relief valve discharge spillage. 36 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
flashcards.explanation_long: Open site drain: A floor drainage receptacle without a strainer or grate capable of receiving surface drainage, com monly used in mechanical rooms for equipment pressure and temperature relief valve discharge spillage. 36 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Open site drain

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sewer receiving the sewage from a collection system and carrying it to the point of final discharge or treatment. It usually is the largest sewer of an entire system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Outfall sewer: A sewer receiving the sewage from a collection system and carrying it to the point of final discharge or treatment. It usually is the largest sewer of an entire system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The actual amount of available material necessary to perform the intended function of a device.
flashcards.explanation_long: Output: The actual amount of available material necessary to perform the intended function of a device.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A redundant (emergency) roof drain installed in roofs with parapet walls that entraps rainwater and removes it from the surface of the roof into a leader, discharging above grade. See also . secondary roof drain
flashcards.explanation_long: Overflow roof drain: A redundant (emergency) roof drain installed in roofs with parapet walls that entraps rainwater and removes it from the surface of the roof into a leader, discharging above grade. See also . secondary roof drain
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Overflow roof drain

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A nonflammable gas that will support combustion.
flashcards.explanation_long: Oxidant: A nonflammable gas that will support combustion.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The maximum design flow rate calculated by multiplying the connected load with the diversity factor.
flashcards.explanation_long: Peak load: The maximum design flow rate calculated by multiplying the connected load with the diversity factor.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Also called infiltration, the rate that a liquid will flow deeper into a soil.
flashcards.explanation_long: Percolation: Also called infiltration, the rate that a liquid will flow deeper into a soil.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The amount of downward slope or grade given to horizontal piping and expressed in inches per foot (mm/m) on a horizontally projected run of pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Pitch: The amount of downward slope or grade given to horizontal piping and expressed in inches per foot (mm/m) on a horizontally projected run of pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The practice, materials, and fixtures used in the installation, maintenance, extension, and alteration of all piping, fixtures, appliances, and plumbing appurtenances within or adjacent to any structure in connection with sanitary or liquid waste drainage, storm drainage facilities, venting systems, public or private water supply systems, and natural and other gases to their connection with any point of supply, public disposal, or other acceptable termi nal. It does not include any fire, product, or process work.
flashcards.explanation_long: Plumbing: The practice, materials, and fixtures used in the installation, maintenance, extension, and alteration of all piping, fixtures, appliances, and plumbing appurtenances within or adjacent to any structure in connection with sanitary or liquid waste drainage, storm drainage facilities, venting systems, public or private water supply systems, and natural and other gases to their connection with any point of supply, public disposal, or other acceptable termi nal. It does not include any fire, product, or process work.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any one of a special class of plumbing fixtures intended to perform a special function, in cluding fixtures having operation and control dependent on one or more energized components, such as motors, controls, heating elements, or pressure or temperature-sensing elements.
flashcards.explanation_long: Plumbing appliance: Any one of a special class of plumbing fixtures intended to perform a special function, in cluding fixtures having operation and control dependent on one or more energized components, such as motors, controls, heating elements, or pressure or temperature-sensing elements.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A manufactured device, prefabricated assembly, or on-the-job assembly of components that is adjunct with the basic piping system and plumbing fixtures.
flashcards.explanation_long: Plumbing appurtenance: A manufactured device, prefabricated assembly, or on-the-job assembly of components that is adjunct with the basic piping system and plumbing fixtures.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The application of scientific principles to the design, installation, and operation of efficient, economical, ecological, energy-conserving, and plumbing code-compliant systems for the transport and distribu tion of liquids and gases.
flashcards.explanation_long: Plumbing engineering: The application of scientific principles to the design, installation, and operation of efficient, economical, ecological, energy-conserving, and plumbing code-compliant systems for the transport and distribu tion of liquids and gases.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An installed receptacle, device, or appliance other than a trap that dispenses potable hot and cold water and discharges the waste into the plumbing drainage system of a facility.
flashcards.explanation_long: Plumbing fixture: An installed receptacle, device, or appliance other than a trap that dispenses potable hot and cold water and discharges the waste into the plumbing drainage system of a facility.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any person who, under the authority of the department having jurisdiction, is authorized to inspect plumbing and drainage systems as defined in the code for the municipality and complies with the laws of licensing and/or registration of the state, city, or county.
flashcards.explanation_long: Plumbing inspector: Any person who, under the authority of the department having jurisdiction, is authorized to inspect plumbing and drainage systems as defined in the code for the municipality and complies with the laws of licensing and/or registration of the state, city, or county.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: All potable water supply and distribution piping, plumbing fixtures and traps, drainage and vent pipe, and natural gas systems including their respective joints, connections, devices, receptacles, and appurtenances within the property lines of the premises, including additional components in the system such as potable water-treating or water-using equipment, fuel gas piping, water heaters, and vents.
flashcards.explanation_long: Plumbing systems: All potable water supply and distribution piping, plumbing fixtures and traps, drainage and vent pipe, and natural gas systems including their respective joints, connections, devices, receptacles, and appurtenances within the property lines of the premises, including additional components in the system such as potable water-treating or water-using equipment, fuel gas piping, water heaters, and vents.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A chemical compound or mixture of compounds of high molecular weight formed by the polymeriza tion of monomers.
flashcards.explanation_long: Polymer: A chemical compound or mixture of compounds of high molecular weight formed by the polymeriza tion of monomers.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The space between particles of soil.
flashcards.explanation_long: Pore: The space between particles of soil.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Water of sufficient purity suitable for human use and meeting the quality standards and regula tions of the public health authority having jurisdiction.
flashcards.explanation_long: Potable water: Water of sufficient purity suitable for human use and meeting the quality standards and regula tions of the public health authority having jurisdiction.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Water directly discharged from the clouds as snow, rain, hail, or sleet.
flashcards.explanation_long: Precipitation: Water directly discharged from the clouds as snow, rain, hail, or sleet.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The estimated maximum force per unit area that a medium in a pipe can exert continuously with a high degree of certainty that failure of the pipe would not occur.
flashcards.explanation_long: Pressure rating: The estimated maximum force per unit area that a medium in a pipe can exert continuously with a high degree of certainty that failure of the pipe would not occur.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A system not connected to any public sewer or point of disposal that discharges effluent into a tank that reduces the sewage to a liquid and discharges this liquid into a subsurface disposal field, one or more seepage pits, a combination of subsurface disposal field and seepage pit, or other such facilities as may be permitted under the procedures set forth in a code.
flashcards.explanation_long: Private sewage disposal system: A system not connected to any public sewer or point of disposal that discharges effluent into a tank that reduces the sewage to a liquid and discharges this liquid into a subsurface disposal field, one or more seepage pits, a combination of subsurface disposal field and seepage pit, or other such facilities as may be permitted under the procedures set forth in a code.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Private sewage disposal system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sewer that is privately owned and not directly operated by public authority.
flashcards.explanation_long: Private sewer: A sewer that is privately owned and not directly operated by public authority.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Plumbing fixtures in residences and apartments, private bathrooms in hotels and hospitals, and re strooms in commercial establishments containing restricted-use single fixtures or groups of single fixtures and similar installations, where the fixtures are intended for the use of a family or an individual.
flashcards.explanation_long: Private use: Plumbing fixtures in residences and apartments, private bathrooms in hotels and hospitals, and re strooms in commercial establishments containing restricted-use single fixtures or groups of single fixtures and similar installations, where the fixtures are intended for the use of a family or an individual.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A common sewer directly operated by the public authority.
flashcards.explanation_long: Public sewer: A common sewer directly operated by the public authority.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Toilet rooms and bathrooms used by employees, occupants, visitors, or patrons in or about any prem ises, and locked toilet rooms or bathrooms to which several occupants or employees on the premises possess keys and have access.
flashcards.explanation_long: Public use: Toilet rooms and bathrooms used by employees, occupants, visitors, or patrons in or about any prem ises, and locked toilet rooms or bathrooms to which several occupants or employees on the premises possess keys and have access.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Biological decomposition of organic matter with the production of ill-smelling products, usually occurring when there is a deficiency of oxygen.
flashcards.explanation_long: Putrefaction: Biological decomposition of organic matter with the production of ill-smelling products, usually occurring when there is a deficiency of oxygen.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Commonly called the rate of rainfall, the amount of rain measured in inches per hour (mm/hour).
flashcards.explanation_long: Rainfall intensity: Commonly called the rate of rainfall, the amount of rain measured in inches per hour (mm/hour).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Untreated sewage.
flashcards.explanation_long: Raw sewage: Untreated sewage.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Nonpotable water used as the intake to any device or process, generally used to describe water supply from a natural source such as a river, lake, or stream.
flashcards.explanation_long: Raw water: Nonpotable water used as the intake to any device or process, generally used to describe water supply from a natural source such as a river, lake, or stream.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A plumbing fixture or device of such material, shape, and capacity that it will adequately receive the dis charge from indirect waste pipes and so constructed and located that it can be readily cleaned.
flashcards.explanation_long: Receptor: A plumbing fixture or device of such material, shape, and capacity that it will adequately receive the dis charge from indirect waste pipes and so constructed and located that it can be readily cleaned.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The amount of water capable of being heated to the design temperature per unit of time.
flashcards.explanation_long: Recovery rate: The amount of water capable of being heated to the design temperature per unit of time.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A backflow prevention device consisting of two independently acting check valves separated by an intermediate chamber, intended to discharge water that backflows into the chamber.
flashcards.explanation_long: Reduced pressure zone backflow preventer: A backflow prevention device consisting of two independently acting check valves separated by an intermediate chamber, intended to discharge water that backflows into the chamber.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Reduced pressure zone backflow preventer

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe fitting larger at one end than at the other used to connect pipes of different diameters.
flashcards.explanation_long: Reducer: A pipe fitting larger at one end than at the other used to connect pipes of different diameters.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A water basin used for decorative purposes.
flashcards.explanation_long: Reflecting pool: A water basin used for decorative purposes.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An individual who is registered or licensed to practice professional architecture or engineering as defined by the statutory requirements of the professional registration laws of the state or jurisdic tion in which the project is to be constructed.
flashcards.explanation_long: Registered design professional: An individual who is registered or licensed to practice professional architecture or engineering as defined by the statutory requirements of the professional registration laws of the state or jurisdic tion in which the project is to be constructed.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Registered design professional

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device intended to reduce a variable inlet pressure to a constant outlet pressure under variable flow conditions.
flashcards.explanation_long: Regulator: A device intended to reduce a variable inlet pressure to a constant outlet pressure under variable flow conditions.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vent designed to equalize the pressure of air between drainage and vent systems or to act as an auxiliary vent.
flashcards.explanation_long: Relief vent: A vent designed to equalize the pressure of air between drainage and vent systems or to act as an auxiliary vent.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Water pressure less than static pressure that varies with flow rate.
flashcards.explanation_long: Residual pressure: Water pressure less than static pressure that varies with flow rate.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A measurement of the resistance of a substance to the passage of an electrical current.
flashcards.explanation_long: Resistivity: A measurement of the resistance of a substance to the passage of an electrical current.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A double offset installed to return a pipe to its original alignment.
flashcards.explanation_long: Return offset: A double offset installed to return a pipe to its original alignment.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The amount of time that must elapse to produce the most severe design storm. Also called frequency.
flashcards.explanation_long: Return period: The amount of time that must elapse to produce the most severe design storm. Also called frequency.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: That part of a vent pipe line that connects directly with an individual waste pipe or group of waste pipes, underneath or at the back of the fixture, and extending either to the main or branch vent pipe Also known . as individual vent.
flashcards.explanation_long: Revent pipe: That part of a vent pipe line that connects directly with an individual waste pipe or group of waste pipes, underneath or at the back of the fixture, and extending either to the main or branch vent pipe Also known . as individual vent.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An unobstructed open edge of a fixture.
flashcards.explanation_long: Rim: An unobstructed open edge of a fixture.
