flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A rough stone of various sizes placed irregularly to prevent scouring or erosion by water or debris.
flashcards.explanation_long: Riprap: A rough stone of various sizes placed irregularly to prevent scouring or erosion by water or debris.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vertical water supply pipe that extends one full story or more to convey water to branches, fixtures, or the fire suppression system above or below grade
flashcards.explanation_long: Riser: A vertical water supply pipe that extends one full story or more to convey water to branches, fixtures, or the fire suppression system above or below grade

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A drain installed to remove water collecting on the surface of a roof and discharge it into the leader.
flashcards.explanation_long: Roof drain: A drain installed to remove water collecting on the surface of a roof and discharge it into the leader.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The installation of all parts of a plumbing system that can be completed prior to the installation of fixtures, including drainage, water supply, and vent piping and the necessary fixture supports.
flashcards.explanation_long: Roughing in: The installation of all parts of a plumbing system that can be completed prior to the installation of fixtures, including drainage, water supply, and vent piping and the necessary fixture supports.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A horizontal drainage line connected to a vertical line at its lowest level.
flashcards.explanation_long: Runout: A horizontal drainage line connected to a vertical line at its lowest level.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A water treatment device for removing solid or colloidal material using sand as the filter medium.
flashcards.explanation_long: Sand filter: A water treatment device for removing solid or colloidal material using sand as the filter medium.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: 1. When used for plumbing, any drainage system that carries human waste. 2. When used for pharma ceutical purposes, a system that is clean or sterile.
flashcards.explanation_long: Sanitary: 1. When used for plumbing, any drainage system that carries human waste. 2. When used for pharma ceutical purposes, a system that is clean or sterile.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A conduit or pipe carrying sanitary sewage. 38 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
flashcards.explanation_long: Sanitary sewer: A conduit or pipe carrying sanitary sewage. 38 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A system of iron pipe sizes that provides for standardized outside diameters and wall thicknesses.
flashcards.explanation_long: Schedule: A system of iron pipe sizes that provides for standardized outside diameters and wall thicknesses.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An opening in a parapet wall above the roof line serving as an overflow.
flashcards.explanation_long: Scupper: An opening in a parapet wall above the roof line serving as an overflow.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A redundant (emergency) roof drain installed in roofs with parapet walls that entraps rainwa ter and removes it from the surface of the roof into a leader, discharging above grade. See also overflow roof drain .
flashcards.explanation_long: Secondary roof drain: A redundant (emergency) roof drain installed in roofs with parapet walls that entraps rainwa ter and removes it from the surface of the roof into a leader, discharging above grade. See also overflow roof drain .
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Secondary roof drain

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An excavation in the ground that receives the discharge of a septic tank and is designed to permit effluent from the tank to seep through its bottom and sides.
flashcards.explanation_long: Seepage pit: An excavation in the ground that receives the discharge of a septic tank and is designed to permit effluent from the tank to seep through its bottom and sides.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The ability of a material to resist burning when the source of heat has been removed.
flashcards.explanation_long: Self-extinguishing: The ability of a material to resist burning when the source of heat has been removed.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A watertight receptor that receives the discharge of a drainage system, or part thereof, designed and constructed to digest organic matter over a period of detention and then discharge the wastewater into the soil.
flashcards.explanation_long: Septic tank: A watertight receptor that receives the discharge of a drainage system, or part thereof, designed and constructed to digest organic matter over a period of detention and then discharge the wastewater into the soil.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A percent number used to reduce the strength value used to obtain an engineered stress.
flashcards.explanation_long: Service factor: A percent number used to reduce the strength value used to obtain an engineered stress.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Hot water for other than potable use, intended for commercial or industrial purposes.
flashcards.explanation_long: Service hot water: Hot water for other than potable use, intended for commercial or industrial purposes.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Service hot water

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any liquid waste containing animal, vegetable, or chemical wastes in suspension or solution.
flashcards.explanation_long: Sewage: Any liquid waste containing animal, vegetable, or chemical wastes in suspension or solution.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device used to lift sewage by entraining it in a high-velocity jet or stream of air or water.
flashcards.explanation_long: Sewage ejector: A device used to lift sewage by entraining it in a high-velocity jet or stream of air or water.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A multiple water connection in the form of a wye on the outside of a building that combines flow from two or more lines into a single pipe supplying water into the building, used for fire department purposes.
flashcards.explanation_long: Siamese connection: A multiple water connection in the form of a wye on the outside of a building that combines flow from two or more lines into a single pipe supplying water into the building, used for fire department purposes.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vertical surface that contributes runoff to the stormwater drainage system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Sidewall area: A vertical surface that contributes runoff to the stormwater drainage system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A fitting used in drainage systems, usually from a fixture, where one pipe slides into another.
flashcards.explanation_long: Slip joint: A fitting used in drainage systems, usually from a fixture, where one pipe slides into another.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The accumulated waste solids of sewage deposited in tanks, beds, or basins, mixed with water to form a semi-liquid mass.
flashcards.explanation_long: Sludge: The accumulated waste solids of sewage deposited in tanks, beds, or basins, mixed with water to form a semi-liquid mass.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any pipe that conveys the discharge of human or animal bodily waste.
flashcards.explanation_long: Soil pipe: Any pipe that conveys the discharge of human or animal bodily waste.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The joining of two pipes using a filler metal that has a melting point less than 1,000°F (538°C).
flashcards.explanation_long: Soldering: The joining of two pipes using a filler metal that has a melting point less than 1,000°F (538°C).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Waste that requires some special method of handling, such as the use of indirect waste piping and receptors, corrosion-resistant piping, sand, oil, or grease interceptors, condensers, or other pretreatment.
flashcards.explanation_long: Special waste: Waste that requires some special method of handling, such as the use of indirect waste piping and receptors, corrosion-resistant piping, sand, oil, or grease interceptors, condensers, or other pretreatment.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The ratio of the weight of one substance to another standard of equal volume. For gas, the standard is air (1), and the standard for liquids and solids is water (1).
flashcards.explanation_long: Specific gravity: The ratio of the weight of one substance to another standard of equal volume. For gas, the standard is air (1), and the standard for liquids and solids is water (1).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The centerline of a buried pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Spring line: The centerline of a buried pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An integrated system of underground and overhead piping designed in accordance with fire-protection engineering standards, including one or more automatic water supplies, which is activated by heat from a fire and discharges water over the fire area.
flashcards.explanation_long: Sprinkler system: An integrated system of underground and overhead piping designed in accordance with fire-protection engineering standards, including one or more automatic water supplies, which is activated by heat from a fire and discharges water over the fire area.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A special-purpose fire-suppression system employing departures from the requirements of standards, such as special water supplies and reduced pipe sizing, installed in accordance with its listing.
flashcards.explanation_long: Sprinkler system, special type: A special-purpose fire-suppression system employing departures from the requirements of standards, such as special water supplies and reduced pipe sizing, installed in accordance with its listing.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Sprinkler system, special type

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A recessed sprinkler with cover plates.
flashcards.explanation_long: Concealed sprinkler: A recessed sprinkler with cover plates.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler with a special coating or plating to be used in an atmosphere that would corrode an uncoated sprinkler.
flashcards.explanation_long: Corrosion-resistant sprinkler: A sprinkler with a special coating or plating to be used in an atmosphere that would corrode an uncoated sprinkler.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler for use in a pendent position in a dry-pipe or wet-pipe system with the seal in a heated area.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dry pendent sprinkler: A sprinkler for use in a pendent position in a dry-pipe or wet-pipe system with the seal in a heated area.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Dry pendent sprinkler

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler designed to be installed in an upright position on a wet-pipe system to extend into an unheated area with a seal in a heated area.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dry upright sprinkler: A sprinkler designed to be installed in an upright position on a wet-pipe system to extend into an unheated area with a seal in a heated area.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Dry upright sprinkler

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler designed to be fast response and listed for its capability to provide fire suppression of a specific high-challenge fire hazard.
flashcards.explanation_long: Early suppression fast response (ESFR) sprinkler: A sprinkler designed to be fast response and listed for its capability to provide fire suppression of a specific high-challenge fire hazard.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Early suppression fast response (ESFR) sprinkler

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler (pendent or sidewall) with special extended, directional discharge patterns.
flashcards.explanation_long: Extended-coverage sprinkler: A sprinkler (pendent or sidewall) with special extended, directional discharge patterns.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler in which all or part of the body, including the shank thread, is mounted above the lower plane of the ceiling.
flashcards.explanation_long: Flush sprinkler: A sprinkler in which all or part of the body, including the shank thread, is mounted above the lower plane of the ceiling.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler equipped with integral shields to protect its operating elements from the discharge of sprinklers installed at higher elevations.
flashcards.explanation_long: Intermediate-level sprinkler: A sprinkler equipped with integral shields to protect its operating elements from the discharge of sprinklers installed at higher elevations.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A listed sprinkler characterized by a K factor between 11.0 and 11.5, large drops of such size and velocity as to enable effective penetration of a high-velocity fire plume, and a proven ability to meet the penetration, cooling, and distribution criteria prescribed in the large-drop sprinkler examination requirements.
flashcards.explanation_long: Large-drop sprinkler: A listed sprinkler characterized by a K factor between 11.0 and 11.5, large drops of such size and velocity as to enable effective penetration of a high-velocity fire plume, and a proven ability to meet the penetration, cooling, and distribution criteria prescribed in the large-drop sprinkler examination requirements.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device for use in applications requiring special discharge patterns, directional spray, fine spray, or other unusual discharge characteristics.
flashcards.explanation_long: Nozzle: A device for use in applications requiring special discharge patterns, directional spray, fine spray, or other unusual discharge characteristics.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler that directs 40 to 60 percent of the total water initially in a downward direction and designed to be installed with a deflector either upright or pendent.
flashcards.explanation_long: Old-style/conventional sprinkler: A sprinkler that directs 40 to 60 percent of the total water initially in a downward direction and designed to be installed with a deflector either upright or pendent.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler from which the actuating elements (fusible links) have been removed.
flashcards.explanation_long: Open sprinkler: A sprinkler from which the actuating elements (fusible links) have been removed.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler that has been painted or plated by the manufacturer.
flashcards.explanation_long: Ornamental sprinkler: A sprinkler that has been painted or plated by the manufacturer.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler designed to be installed in such a way that the water stream is directed downward against the deflector. (Note the spelling of the word pendent a convention adopted by the fire protection industry.)
flashcards.explanation_long: Pendent sprinkler: A sprinkler designed to be installed in such a way that the water stream is directed downward against the deflector. (Note the spelling of the word pendent a convention adopted by the fire protection industry.)

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A quick response sprinkler that complies with the extended protection areas as defined by NFPA and tested by the manufacturer.
flashcards.explanation_long: Quick response early suppression (QRES) sprinkler: A quick response sprinkler that complies with the extended protection areas as defined by NFPA and tested by the manufacturer.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Quick response early suppression (QRES) sprinkler

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A type of sprinkler that is both a fast-response and a spray sprinkler.
flashcards.explanation_long: Quick-response (QR) sprinkler: A type of sprinkler that is both a fast-response and a spray sprinkler.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler in which all or a part of the body, other than the shank thread, is mounted within a recessed housing.
flashcards.explanation_long: Recessed sprinkler: A sprinkler in which all or a part of the body, other than the shank thread, is mounted within a recessed housing.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler that has been specifically listed for use in residential occupancies.
flashcards.explanation_long: Residential sprinkler: A sprinkler that has been specifically listed for use in residential occupancies.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler having special deflectors that are designed to discharge most of the water away from a nearby wall in a pattern resembling a quarter of a sphere, with a small portion of the discharge directed at the wall behind the sprinkler.
flashcards.explanation_long: Sidewall sprinkler: A sprinkler having special deflectors that are designed to discharge most of the water away from a nearby wall in a pattern resembling a quarter of a sphere, with a small portion of the discharge directed at the wall behind the sprinkler.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler that has been tested and listed as having special limitations.
flashcards.explanation_long: Special sprinkler: A sprinkler that has been tested and listed as having special limitations.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sprinkler designed to be installed in such a way that the water spray is directed upward against the deflector.
flashcards.explanation_long: Upright sprinkler: A sprinkler designed to be installed in such a way that the water spray is directed upward against the deflector.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The vertical main pipe of a system of soil, waste, or vent piping extending through one or more stories.
flashcards.explanation_long: Stack: The vertical main pipe of a system of soil, waste, or vent piping extending through one or more stories.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The extension of a soil waste stack above the highest horizontal drain connected to the stack extend ing to the outside air.
flashcards.explanation_long: Stack vent: The extension of a soil waste stack above the highest horizontal drain connected to the stack extend ing to the outside air.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A method of venting a fixture or fixtures through a soil or waste stack.
flashcards.explanation_long: Stack venting: A method of venting a fixture or fixtures through a soil or waste stack.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A generally accepted document, published by a recognized authority, that is referenced by inclusion in codes and requires conformance.
flashcards.explanation_long: Standard: A generally accepted document, published by a recognized authority, that is referenced by inclusion in codes and requires conformance.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Regarding plastic pipe, the diameter of the pipe divided by the wall thickness.
flashcards.explanation_long: Standard dimensional ratio: Regarding plastic pipe, the diameter of the pipe divided by the wall thickness.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Standard dimensional ratio

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Conforming to the temperature and pressure requirements of the authority having jurisdiction.
flashcards.explanation_long: Standard temperature and pressure: Conforming to the temperature and pressure requirements of the authority having jurisdiction.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Standard temperature and pressure

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vertical pipe generally used for the distribution of water for fire extinguishing.
flashcards.explanation_long: Standpipe: A vertical pipe generally used for the distribution of water for fire extinguishing.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An arrangement of piping, valves, hose connections, and allied equipment with a connection to a water supply installed in a building or structure in such a manner that water can be discharged in streams or spray patterns through attached hoses and nozzles, for the purpose of extinguishing a fire and protecting a building or structure and its contents as well as its occupants.
flashcards.explanation_long: Standpipe system: An arrangement of piping, valves, hose connections, and allied equipment with a connection to a water supply installed in a building or structure in such a manner that water can be discharged in streams or spray patterns through attached hoses and nozzles, for the purpose of extinguishing a fire and protecting a building or structure and its contents as well as its occupants.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: For use by fire departments and those trained in handling heavy fire streams (2½-inch hose).
flashcards.explanation_long: Class I: For use by fire departments and those trained in handling heavy fire streams (2½-inch hose).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: For use primarily by the building occupants until the arrival of the fire department (1½-inch hose).
flashcards.explanation_long: Class II: For use primarily by the building occupants until the arrival of the fire department (1½-inch hose).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: For use either by fire departments and those trained in handling heavy hose streams (2½-inch hose) or by the building occupants (1½-inch hose). 40 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
flashcards.explanation_long: Class III: For use either by fire departments and those trained in handling heavy hose streams (2½-inch hose) or by the building occupants (1½-inch hose). 40 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A system having no permanent water supply, arranged through the use of approved devices to admit water to the system automatically by the opening of a hose valve.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dry standpipe: A system having no permanent water supply, arranged through the use of approved devices to admit water to the system automatically by the opening of a hose valve.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A system with an open supply valve and pressurized water in the system at all times.
flashcards.explanation_long: Wet standpipe: A system with an open supply valve and pressurized water in the system at all times.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Commonly called an angle stop, a valve used to control the water supply, usually to a single fixture.
flashcards.explanation_long: Stop valve: Commonly called an angle stop, a valve used to control the water supply, usually to a single fixture.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A sewer used for conveying only rainwater, snow, ice, or other natural precipitation.
flashcards.explanation_long: Storm sewer: A sewer used for conveying only rainwater, snow, ice, or other natural precipitation.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Rainwater, snow, ice, or other natural precipitation from site or roof surfaces.
flashcards.explanation_long: Stormwater: Rainwater, snow, ice, or other natural precipitation from site or roof surfaces.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Change in the shape or size of a body produced by the action of stress.
flashcards.explanation_long: Strain: Change in the shape or size of a body produced by the action of stress.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A condition found inside water heating tanks where water remains in layers depending on tempera ture instead of mixing.
flashcards.explanation_long: Stratification: A condition found inside water heating tanks where water remains in layers depending on tempera ture instead of mixing.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The pressure available in a public water main.
flashcards.explanation_long: Street pressure: The pressure available in a public water main.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Reactions within a body resisting external forces acting on it.
flashcards.explanation_long: Stress: Reactions within a body resisting external forces acting on it.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A drain that receives only subsurface or seepage water and conveys it to an approved place of disposal.
flashcards.explanation_long: Subsoil drain: A drain that receives only subsurface or seepage water and conveys it to an approved place of disposal.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The portion of a waste stack where the formation of soap suds could create pressure higher than atmospheric pressure.
flashcards.explanation_long: Suds pressure zone: The portion of a waste stack where the formation of soap suds could create pressure higher than atmospheric pressure.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Suds pressure zone

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A tank or pit located below the normal grade of a gravity system that receives clear liquid waste and must be emptied by mechanical means.
flashcards.explanation_long: Sump: A tank or pit located below the normal grade of a gravity system that receives clear liquid waste and must be emptied by mechanical means.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A mechanical device for removing clear liquid waste from a sump.
flashcards.explanation_long: Sump pump: A mechanical device for removing clear liquid waste from a sump.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: When many flush valves are used at one time in stadiums and places of assembly.
flashcards.explanation_long: Super flush: When many flush valves are used at one time in stadiums and places of assembly.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device attached to the handle of a valve that, when the valve is operated, annunci ates a trouble signal at a remote location.
flashcards.explanation_long: Supervisory (tamper) switch: A device attached to the handle of a valve that, when the valve is operated, annunci ates a trouble signal at a remote location.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device for supporting and securing pipe, fixtures, and equipment to walls, ceilings, floors, or structural members.
flashcards.explanation_long: Support: A device for supporting and securing pipe, fixtures, and equipment to walls, ceilings, floors, or structural members.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A structure, basin, or tank containing water for swimming, diving, or recreation.
flashcards.explanation_long: Swimming pool: A structure, basin, or tank containing water for swimming, diving, or recreation.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A tee is a fitting where a straight run of pipe has a connection at right angle to the run.
flashcards.explanation_long: Tee: A tee is a fitting where a straight run of pipe has a connection at right angle to the run.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A measure of heat.
flashcards.explanation_long: Temperature: A measure of heat.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An incremental value in metric units (SI) where the freezing point of water (0°C) and the boiling point of water (100°C) are divided into 100 divisions.
flashcards.explanation_long: Degree Celsius (formally Centigrade) (C): An incremental value in metric units (SI) where the freezing point of water (0°C) and the boiling point of water (100°C) are divided into 100 divisions.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Degree Celsius (formally Centigrade) (C)

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An incremental value in English (IP) units, where the freezing point of water (32°F) and the boiling point of water (212°F) are divided into 180 divisions.
flashcards.explanation_long: Degree Fahrenheit (F): An incremental value in English (IP) units, where the freezing point of water (32°F) and the boiling point of water (212°F) are divided into 180 divisions.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A value in degrees Celsius based on a starting temperature of absolute zero, 273.15°C, created for laboratory use.
flashcards.explanation_long: Degree Kelvin (K): A value in degrees Celsius based on a starting temperature of absolute zero, 273.15°C, created for laboratory use.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A value in degrees Fahrenheit based on a starting temperature of absolute zero, 459.67°F.
flashcards.explanation_long: Degree Rankine (R): A value in degrees Fahrenheit based on a starting temperature of absolute zero, 459.67°F.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Hot and cold water mixed to obtain an intermediate temperature, generally from 80°F to 100°F (29°C to 43°C).
flashcards.explanation_long: Tempered water: Hot and cold water mixed to obtain an intermediate temperature, generally from 80°F to 100°F (29°C to 43°C).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A moderate, lukewarm water temperature, between 60°F and 100°F (16°C and 38°C).
flashcards.explanation_long: Tepid: A moderate, lukewarm water temperature, between 60°F and 100°F (16°C and 38°C).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A ratio of the energy output from a system to the energy input to the system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Thermal efficiency: A ratio of the energy output from a system to the energy input to the system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Certification obtained by the manufacturer indicating that the function and performance characteristics of a product or material have been determined by testing and ongoing surveillance by an approved third-party certification agency.
flashcards.explanation_long: Third-party certified: Certification obtained by the manufacturer indicating that the function and performance characteristics of a product or material have been determined by testing and ongoing surveillance by an approved third-party certification agency.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A heavy, solid material placed at a fitting of an underground water pipe on undisturbed soil, used to resist the force generated by flowing water on the fitting.
flashcards.explanation_long: Thrust block: A heavy, solid material placed at a fitting of an underground water pipe on undisturbed soil, used to resist the force generated by flowing water on the fitting.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The suggested international standard term to replace millimeters of mercury.
flashcards.explanation_long: Torr: The suggested international standard term to replace millimeters of mercury.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The ability of a substance to produce injurious or lethal effects on a susceptible site.
flashcards.explanation_long: Toxic: The ability of a substance to produce injurious or lethal effects on a susceptible site.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The part of the horizontal piping of a drainage system that begins 2 feet (0.6 m) downstream from the last trailer site connection, receives the discharge of the trailer site, and conveys it to a public sewer, private sewer, individual sewage disposal system, or other approved point of disposal.
flashcards.explanation_long: Trailer park sewer: The part of the horizontal piping of a drainage system that begins 2 feet (0.6 m) downstream from the last trailer site connection, receives the discharge of the trailer site, and conveys it to a public sewer, private sewer, individual sewage disposal system, or other approved point of disposal.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Trailer park sewer

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A fitting or device designed and constructed to provide, when properly vented, a liquid seal that will prevent the back passage of air without significantly affecting the flow of sewage or wastewater through it.
flashcards.explanation_long: Trap: A fitting or device designed and constructed to provide, when properly vented, a liquid seal that will prevent the back passage of air without significantly affecting the flow of sewage or wastewater through it.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device or system of piping to maintain a water seal in a trap.
flashcards.explanation_long: Trap primer: A device or system of piping to maintain a water seal in a trap.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The maximum vertical depth of liquid that a trap will retain, measured between the crown weir and the top of the dip of the trap.
flashcards.explanation_long: Trap seal: The maximum vertical depth of liquid that a trap will retain, measured between the crown weir and the top of the dip of the trap.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The temperature and pressure for a pure substance where the three phases (liquid, solid, and gas) exist in equilibrium.
flashcards.explanation_long: Triple point: The temperature and pressure for a pure substance where the three phases (liquid, solid, and gas) exist in equilibrium.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The room necessary to remove either heating tube bundle from a water heater.
flashcards.explanation_long: Tube pull: The room necessary to remove either heating tube bundle from a water heater.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A measure of the number of suspended particles in a liquid.
flashcards.explanation_long: Turbidity: A measure of the number of suspended particles in a liquid.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any deviation from parallel flow in a pipe.
flashcards.explanation_long: Turbulence: Any deviation from parallel flow in a pipe.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Pipe buried below grade.
flashcards.explanation_long: Underground piping: Pipe buried below grade.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A condition that is contrary to sanitary principles or that is injurious to health.
flashcards.explanation_long: Unsanitary: A condition that is contrary to sanitary principles or that is injurious to health.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A location in the direction of flow before reaching a referenced point.
flashcards.explanation_long: Upstream: A location in the direction of flow before reaching a referenced point.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any pressure less than that exerted by the atmosphere.
flashcards.explanation_long: Vacuum: Any pressure less than that exerted by the atmosphere.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vacuum breaker not designed to be subject to static line pressure.
flashcards.explanation_long: Vacuum breaker, atmospheric: A vacuum breaker not designed to be subject to static line pressure.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Vacuum breaker, atmospheric

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vacuum breaker designed to operate under static line pressure.
flashcards.explanation_long: Vacuum breaker, pressure: A vacuum breaker designed to operate under static line pressure.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Vacuum breaker, pressure

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device used to prevent excessive vacuum in a pressure vessel.
flashcards.explanation_long: Vacuum relief valve: A device used to prevent excessive vacuum in a pressure vessel.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Vacuum relief valve

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A fitting whose primary function is to control flow inside a pipe by means of a movable closure member.
flashcards.explanation_long: Valve: A fitting whose primary function is to control flow inside a pipe by means of a movable closure member.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The pressure characteristics at any given temperature of a vapor in equilibrium with its liquid.
flashcards.explanation_long: Vapor pressure: The pressure characteristics at any given temperature of a vapor in equilibrium with its liquid.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Flow rate measured in feet per second (m/sec).
flashcards.explanation_long: Velocity: Flow rate measured in feet per second (m/sec).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any vent connecting a horizontal branch or fixture drain with the stack vent of the originating waste or soil stack.
flashcards.explanation_long: Vent, loop: Any vent connecting a horizontal branch or fixture drain with the stack vent of the originating waste or soil stack.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vertical vent pipe installed primarily for the purpose of providing circulation of air to and from any part of the drainage system.
flashcards.explanation_long: Vent stack: A vertical vent pipe installed primarily for the purpose of providing circulation of air to and from any part of the drainage system.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any pipe or fitting installed in a vertical position or that makes an angle of not more than 45 degrees with the vertical.
flashcards.explanation_long: Vertical pipe: Any pipe or fitting installed in a vertical position or that makes an angle of not more than 45 degrees with the vertical.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Fired and glazed earthenware.
flashcards.explanation_long: Vitrified clay: Fired and glazed earthenware.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A faucet on the exterior of a building for the purpose of supplying potable water.
flashcards.explanation_long: Wall hydrant: A faucet on the exterior of a building for the purpose of supplying potable water.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The discharge from any fixture, appliance, area, or appurtenance that does not contain fecal matter.
flashcards.explanation_long: Waste: The discharge from any fixture, appliance, area, or appurtenance that does not contain fecal matter.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The process of capturing and carrying away gases vented from a patient breathing circuit during the normal operation of gas anesthesia or analgesia equipment.
flashcards.explanation_long: Waste anesthetic gas disposal (WAGD): The process of capturing and carrying away gases vented from a patient breathing circuit during the normal operation of gas anesthesia or analgesia equipment.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Waste anesthetic gas disposal (WAGD)

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The discharge pipe from any fixture, appliance, or appurtenance in connection with the plumbing system that does not contain fecal matter.
flashcards.explanation_long: Waste pipe: The discharge pipe from any fixture, appliance, or appurtenance in connection with the plumbing system that does not contain fecal matter.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device that conditions or treats a water supply to change its chemical content or remove suspended solids by filtration.
flashcards.explanation_long: Water-conditioning or treating device: A device that conditions or treats a water supply to change its chemical content or remove suspended solids by filtration.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Water-conditioning or treating device

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any pipe that conveys potable water.
flashcards.explanation_long: Water-distributing pipe: Any pipe that conveys potable water.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A surge pressure resulting from a sudden start or stop of water.
flashcards.explanation_long: Water hammer: A surge pressure resulting from a sudden start or stop of water.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device, other than an air chamber, designed to protect against excessive surge pressure.
flashcards.explanation_long: Water hammer arrester: A device, other than an air chamber, designed to protect against excessive surge pressure.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Water hammer arrester

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The water supply pipe for public or community use, normally under the jurisdiction of the munici pality or water company.
flashcards.explanation_long: Water main: The water supply pipe for public or community use, normally under the jurisdiction of the munici pality or water company.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A water supply pipe that extends vertically one full story or more to convey water to branches or fixtures.
flashcards.explanation_long: Water riser: A water supply pipe that extends vertically one full story or more to convey water to branches or fixtures.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The depth of water in a fixture trap that prevents the passage of noxious odors but allows the free flow of wastewater.
flashcards.explanation_long: Water seal: The depth of water in a fixture trap that prevents the passage of noxious odors but allows the free flow of wastewater.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The pipe from the water main or other source of water supply to the building served. 42 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
flashcards.explanation_long: Water service pipe: The pipe from the water main or other source of water supply to the building served. 42 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Water service pipe

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The building supply pipe, water-distributing pipes, and necessary connecting pipes, fittings, control valves, and all appurtenances carrying or supplying potable water in, or adjacent to, a building or premises.
flashcards.explanation_long: Water supply system: The building supply pipe, water-distributing pipes, and necessary connecting pipes, fittings, control valves, and all appurtenances carrying or supplying potable water in, or adjacent to, a building or premises.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Water supply system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A vent that also serves as a drain.
flashcards.explanation_long: Wet vent: A vent that also serves as a drain.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe connecting upward from a soil or waste stack to a vent stack for the purpose of preventing pres sure changes in the stacks. National Fire Protection Association. NFPA: National Fire Protection Association NSF: National Sanitation Foundation PDI: Plumbing and Drainage Institute PMI: Plumbing Manufacturers International UL: Underwriters Laboratory USGBC: United States Green Building Council WPC: World Plumbing Council WQA: Water Quality Association APPENDIX 1-B PLASTIC PIPING ACRONYMS Following is a list of commonly used and available plastic pipe and elastomer materials from various sources. The names in parentheses are trade names patented by various manufacturers. Elastomers, indicated by (E), are listed only for reference. ABS: Acrylonitrile butadiene styrene BR: Butadiene (E) CA: Cellulose acetate CAB: Cellulose acetate butyrate (Celcon) CAP: Cellulose acetate propionate CIIR: Chlorinated isobutene isoprene (E) CMC: Carboxymethyl cellulose CN: Cellulose nitrate CP: Cellulose propionate CPE: Chlorinated polyethylene (E) CPVC: Chlorinated polyvinyl chloride CR: Chloroprene rubber (Neoprene) (E) CS: Casein CSP: Chlorine sulphonyl polyethylene (Hypalon) (E) ECTFE: Ethylenechlorotrifluoroethylene EP: Epoxide, epoxy EPDM: Ethylene propylene-diene monomer (E) FPM: Fluorine rubber (E) EPM: Ethylene propylene terpolymer (E) FPM: Flurine rubber (Viton) (E) HDPE: High-density polyethylene IIR: Isobutene isoprene (butyl) rubber (E) IR: Polyisopryne (E) PA: Polyamide PAEK: Polyaryl etherketone PB: Polybutylene PC: Polycarbonate PCTFE: Polychlorotrifluoroethylene (Halar) PDAP: Polydiallyl phthalate PE: Polyethylene PEEK: Polyether etherketone PEX: Cross-linked polyethylene PF: Phenol formaldehyde PFA: Perfuoroalkoxy PIB: Polyisobutylene PP: Polypropylene PPS: Polyphenylene sulfide 44 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1 PS: Polysulfone PTFE: Polytetrafluoroethylene (Teflon) PVC: Polyvinyl chloride PVDC: Polyvinylidene chloride PVDF: Polyvinylidene fluoride PVFM: Polyvinyl formal PVK: Polyvinyl carbazol SBR: Styrene butadiene (E) APPENDIX 1-C RECOMMENDED PRACTICE FOR CONVERSION TO THE INTERNATIONAL SYSTEM OF UNITS The International System of Units was developed by the General Conference of Weights and Measures, an inter national treaty organization, and has been officially abbreviated SI from the French term Système International d’Unités. The SI system of units is a preferred international measurement system that evolved from earlier decimal metric systems. When President Ford signed the Metric Conversion Act (Public Law 94-168) on December 23, 1975, a metric system in the United States was declared, and a United States Metric Board was established to coordinate the national voluntary conversion effort to the metric system. The Metric Conversion Act specifically defines the metric system of measurement to be used as the International System of Units (SI), established by the General Conference of Weights and Measures and as interpreted and modified by the Secretary of Commerce. The recommended practice section that follows outlines a selection of SI units, including multiples and sub multiples, for use in plumbing design and related fields of science and engineering. It is intended to provide the technical basis for a comprehensive and authoritative standard guide for SI units to be used in plumbing design and related fields of science and engineering. The section also is intended to provide the basic concepts and practices for the conversion of units given in several systems of measurement to the SI system. Rules and recommendations are detailed for the presentation of SI units and their corresponding symbols and numerical values used in conjunction with the SI system. A selection of conversion factors to SI units for use in plumbing design and related fields of science and engi neering is also given. It should be noted that the SI units, rules, and recommendations listed herein comply with those provisions set forth in IEEE/ASTM SI 10: American National Standard for Metric Practice.
flashcards.explanation_long: Yoke vent: A pipe connecting upward from a soil or waste stack to a vent stack for the purpose of preventing pres sure changes in the stacks. The SI system of units is a preferred international measurement system that evolved from earlier decimal metric systems. When President Ford signed the Metric Conversion Act (Public Law 94-168) on December 23, 1975, a metric system in the United States was declared, and a United States Metric Board was established to coordinate the national voluntary conversion effort to the metric system. The Metric Conversion Act specifically defines the metric system of measurement to be used as the International System of Units (SI), established by the General Conference of Weights and Measures and as interpreted and modified by the Secretary of Commerce. The recommended practice section that follows outlines a selection of SI units, including multiples and sub multiples, for use in plumbing design and related fields of science and engineering. flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: For uniformity in the interpretation of the provisions set forth in this recommended practice section, the fol lowing definitions and abbreviations will apply.
flashcards.explanation_long: Terminology and Abbreviations: For uniformity in the interpretation of the provisions set forth in this recommended practice section, the fol lowing definitions and abbreviations will apply.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Terminology and Abbreviations

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The degree of conformity of a measured or calculated value to some recognized standard or specified value.
flashcards.explanation_long: Accuracy: The degree of conformity of a measured or calculated value to some recognized standard or specified value.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A quantity that is nearly, but not exactly, correct or accurate. CGPM Acronym for the General Conference on Weights and Measures, from the French term Conference Generale de Poids et Measures.
flashcards.explanation_long: Approximate value: A quantity that is nearly, but not exactly, correct or accurate. CGPM Acronym for the General Conference on Weights and Measures, from the French term Conference Generale de Poids et Measures.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A system in which relations between units contain as numerical factor only the number 1 (or unity). All derived units have a unity relationship to the constituent base or supplementary units.
flashcards.explanation_long: Coherent unit system: A system in which relations between units contain as numerical factor only the number 1 (or unity). All derived units have a unity relationship to the constituent base or supplementary units.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Coherent unit system

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The variation from a specified dimension or design requirement, defining the upper and lower limits.
flashcards.explanation_long: Deviation: The variation from a specified dimension or design requirement, defining the upper and lower limits.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: One of the 10 arabic numerals (0 to 9).
flashcards.explanation_long: Digit: One of the 10 arabic numerals (0 to 9).

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A geometric element in a design or the magnitude of such a quantity.
flashcards.explanation_long: Dimension: A geometric element in a design or the magnitude of such a quantity.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An individual characteristic of a component or part.
flashcards.explanation_long: Feature: An individual characteristic of a component or part.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A value assigned for the purpose of convenient designation, existing in name only.
flashcards.explanation_long: Nominal value: A value assigned for the purpose of convenient designation, existing in name only.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The degree of mutual agreement between individual measurements, namely repeatability and repro ducibility.
flashcards.explanation_long: Precision: The degree of mutual agreement between individual measurements, namely repeatability and repro ducibility.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any digit necessary to define a value or quantity.
flashcards.explanation_long: Significant digit: Any digit necessary to define a value or quantity.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The total range of variation permitted; the upper and lower limits between which a dimension must be maintained.
flashcards.explanation_long: Tolerance: The total range of variation permitted; the upper and lower limits between which a dimension must be maintained.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The reference value of a given quantity as defined by CGPM.
flashcards.explanation_long: Unit: The reference value of a given quantity as defined by CGPM.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: These conversions denote the precise (or direct) conversion to the SI unit value, accurate to a number of decimal places.
flashcards.explanation_long: Exact: These conversions denote the precise (or direct) conversion to the SI unit value, accurate to a number of decimal places.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: These conversions denote the conversion to the SI unit value in the software only. The materials and products remain unchanged, and minimal rounding off to the nearest integer is usually applied.
flashcards.explanation_long: Soft: These conversions denote the conversion to the SI unit value in the software only. The materials and products remain unchanged, and minimal rounding off to the nearest integer is usually applied.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: These conversions denote that the product or material characteristics are physically changed from existing values to preferred SI unit values.
flashcards.explanation_long: Hard: These conversions denote that the product or material characteristics are physically changed from existing values to preferred SI unit values.

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The International System of Units has three types of units, as follows.
flashcards.explanation_long: SI Units and Symbols: The International System of Units has three types of units, as follows.
flashcards.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | SI Units and Symbols

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: These units are used for independent quantities. There are seven base units: Quantity Unit Symbol Length meter m Mass kilogram kg Time second s Current (electric) ampere A Temperature (thermodynamic) Kelvin K Substance (amount) mole mol Intensity (luminous) candela cd
flashcards.explanation_long: Base units: These units are used for independent quantities. There are seven base units: Quantity Unit Symbol Length meter m Mass kilogram kg Time second s Current (electric) ampere A Temperature (thermodynamic) Kelvin K Substance (amount) mole mol Intensity (luminous) candela cd

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: These units are used to denote angles. There are two supplementary units: Quantity Unit Symbol Plane angle radian rad Solid angle steradian sr
flashcards.explanation_long: Supplementary units: These units are used to denote angles. There are two supplementary units: Quantity Unit Symbol Plane angle radian rad Solid angle steradian sr

flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: These units are defined in terms of their derivation from base and supplementary units. Derived units are classified in two categories: (1) derived units with special names and symbols and (2) derived units with generic or complex names, expressed in terms of a base unit, two or more base units, base units and/or derived units with special names, or supplementary units and base and/or derived units. flashcards.front: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The SI unit system is based on multiples and submultiples. identification.prompt: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The withstanding of any material to rubbing, scratching, or wearing away.
identification.explanation_long: Abrasion: The withstanding of any material to rubbing, scratching, or wearing away.

identification.prompt: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The total pressure equal to that measured from an absolute vacuum. It equals the sum of gauge pressure plus barometric atmospheric pressure. It is expressed in pounds per square inch (psia) or kilopascals per square meter (kPa/m 2 ).
identification.explanation_long: Absolute pressure: The total pressure equal to that measured from an absolute vacuum. It equals the sum of gauge pressure plus barometric atmospheric pressure. It is expressed in pounds per square inch (psia) or kilopascals per square meter (kPa/m 2 ).

identification.prompt: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Temperature measured from absolute zero.
identification.explanation_long: Absolute temperature: Temperature measured from absolute zero.

identification.prompt: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The point at which any substance has no molecular motion and no heat. It is equivalent to 459.72°F or 273.18°C.
identification.explanation_long: Absolute zero: The point at which any substance has no molecular motion and no heat. It is equivalent to 459.72°F or 273.18°C.

identification.prompt: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The soaking up of a gas or liquid into a solid substance.
identification.explanation_long: Absorption: The soaking up of a gas or liquid into a solid substance.

identification.prompt: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A panel that can be opened and used to provide easy approach to concealed valves or equipment.
identification.explanation_long: Access door: A panel that can be opened and used to provide easy approach to concealed valves or equipment.

identification.prompt: Which plumbing term matches this definition: That which enables a fixture, device, or appliance to be easily reached.
identification.explanation_long: Access to: That which enables a fixture, device, or appliance to be easily reached.

identification.explanation_long: Accuracy: The degree of agreement between a measured value and the true value of a quantity or concern.

identification.prompt: Which plumbing term matches this definition: That which enables direct approach, either with or without the removing or moving of any panel, door, or similar obstruction. identification.explanation_long: Accessible: That which enables direct approach, either with or without the removing or moving of any panel, door, or similar obstruction. Regarding physically challenged individuals, a plumbing fixture, site, building, facility, or portion thereof that can be approached, entered, and/or used.

APPENDIX 1-A PLUMBING ACRONYMS ADA: Americans with Disabilities Act AGA: American Gas Association AHJ: Authority having jurisdiction ANSI: American National Standards Institute ASHRAE: American Society of Heating, Refrigerating, and Air-Conditioning Engineers ASME: American Society of Mechanical Engineers ASPE: American Society of Plumbing Engineers ASPERF: American Society of Plumbing Engineers Research Foundation ASSE: American Society of Sanitary Engineering or American Society of Safety Engineers ASTM: American Society for Testing and Materials AWWA: American Water Works Association AWS : American Welding Society CDA: Copper Development Association CGPM: General Conference on Weights and Measures, from the French term Conference Generale de Poids et Measures CISPI: Cast Iron Soil Pipe Institute CSA: Canadian Standards Association CS: Commercial standards DWV: Drain, waste, and vent FM: Factory Mutual FS: Federal specifications IAPMO: International Association of Plumbing and Mechanical Officials ICC: International Code Council LEED: Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design MSS: Manufacturers Standardization Society of the Valve and Fittings Industry Inc. NFPA: National Fire Protection Association NSF: National Sanitation Foundation PDI: Plumbing and Drainage Institute PMI: Plumbing Manufacturers International UL: Underwriters Laboratory USGBC: United States Green Building Council WPC: World Plumbing Council WQA: Water Quality Association APPENDIX 1-B PLASTIC PIPING ACRONYMS Following is a list of commonly used and available plastic pipe and elastomer materials from various sources. The names in parentheses are trade names patented by various manufacturers. Elastomers, indicated by (E), are listed only for reference. ABS: Acrylonitrile butadiene styrene BR: Butadiene (E) CA: Cellulose acetate CAB: Cellulose acetate butyrate (Celcon) CAP: Cellulose acetate propionate CIIR: Chlorinated isobutene isoprene (E) CMC: Carboxymethyl cellulose CN: Cellulose nitrate CP: Cellulose propionate CPE: Chlorinated polyethylene (E) CPVC: Chlorinated polyvinyl chloride CR: Chloroprene rubber (Neoprene) (E) CS: Casein CSP: Chlorine sulphonyl polyethylene (Hypalon) (E) ECTFE: Ethylenechlorotrifluoroethylene EP: Epoxide, epoxy EPDM: Ethylene propylene-diene monomer (E) FPM: Fluorine rubber (E) EPM: Ethylene propylene terpolymer (E) FPM: Flurine rubber (Viton) (E) HDPE: High-density polyethylene IIR: Isobutene isoprene (butyl) rubber (E) IR: Polyisopryne (E) PA: Polyamide PAEK: Polyaryl etherketone PB: Polybutylene PC: Polycarbonate PCTFE: Polychlorotrifluoroethylene (Halar) PDAP: Polydiallyl phthalate PE: Polyethylene PEEK: Polyether etherketone PEX: Cross-linked polyethylene PF: Phenol formaldehyde PFA: Perfuoroalkoxy PIB: Polyisobutylene PP: Polypropylene PPS: Polyphenylene sulfide 44 ASPE Plumbing Engineering Design Handbook Volume 1 PS: Polysulfone PTFE: Polytetrafluoroethylene (Teflon) PVC: Polyvinyl chloride PVDC: Polyvinylidene chloride PVDF: Polyvinylidene fluoride PVFM: Polyvinyl formal PVK: Polyvinyl carbazol SBR: Styrene butadiene (E) APPENDIX 1-C RECOMMENDED PRACTICE FOR CONVERSION TO THE INTERNATIONAL SYSTEM OF UNITS The International System of Units was developed by the General Conference of Weights and Measures, an inter national treaty organization, and has been officially abbreviated SI from the French term Système International d’Unités. The SI system of units is a preferred international measurement system that evolved from earlier decimal metric systems. When President Ford signed the Metric Conversion Act (Public Law 94-168) on December 23, 1975, a metric system in the United States was declared, and a United States Metric Board was established to coordinate the national voluntary conversion effort to the metric system. The Metric Conversion Act specifically defines the metric system of measurement to be used as the International System of Units (SI), established by the General Conference of Weights and Measures and as interpreted and modified by the Secretary of Commerce. The recommended practice section that follows outlines a selection of SI units, including multiples and sub multiples, for use in plumbing design and related fields of science and engineering. It is intended to provide the technical basis for a comprehensive and authoritative standard guide for SI units to be used in plumbing design and related fields of science and engineering. The section also is intended to provide the basic concepts and practices for the conversion of units given in several systems of measurement to the SI system. Rules and recommendations are detailed for the presentation of SI units and their corresponding symbols and numerical values used in conjunction with the SI system. A selection of conversion factors to SI units for use in plumbing design and related fields of science and engi neering is also given. It should be noted that the SI units, rules, and recommendations listed herein comply with those provisions set forth in IEEE/ASTM SI 10: American National Standard for Metric Practice.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The withstanding of any material to rubbing, scratching, or wearing away.
mcqs.explanation_long: Abrasion: The withstanding of any material to rubbing, scratching, or wearing away.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The total pressure equal to that measured from an absolute vacuum. It equals the sum of gauge pressure plus barometric atmospheric pressure. It is expressed in pounds per square inch (psia) or kilopascals per square meter (kPa/m 2 ).
mcqs.explanation_long: Absolute pressure: The total pressure equal to that measured from an absolute vacuum. It equals the sum of gauge pressure plus barometric atmospheric pressure. It is expressed in pounds per square inch (psia) or kilopascals per square meter (kPa/m 2 ).

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Temperature measured from absolute zero.
mcqs.explanation_long: Absolute temperature: Temperature measured from absolute zero.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The point at which any substance has no molecular motion and no heat. It is equivalent to 459.72°F or 273.18°C.
mcqs.explanation_long: Absolute zero: The point at which any substance has no molecular motion and no heat. It is equivalent to 459.72°F or 273.18°C.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The soaking up of a gas or liquid into a solid substance.
mcqs.explanation_long: Absorption: The soaking up of a gas or liquid into a solid substance.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A panel that can be opened and used to provide easy approach to concealed valves or equipment.
mcqs.explanation_long: Access door: A panel that can be opened and used to provide easy approach to concealed valves or equipment.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: That which enables a fixture, device, or appliance to be easily reached.
mcqs.explanation_long: Access to: That which enables a fixture, device, or appliance to be easily reached.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The degree of agreement between a measured value and the true value of a quantity or concern.
mcqs.explanation_long: Accuracy: The degree of agreement between a measured value and the true value of a quantity or concern.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: That which enables direct approach, either with or without the removing or moving of any panel, door, or similar obstruction. Regarding physically challenged individuals, a plumbing fixture, site, building, facility, or portion thereof that can be approached, entered, and/or used.
mcqs.explanation_long: Accessible: That which enables direct approach, either with or without the removing or moving of any panel, door, or similar obstruction. Regarding physically challenged individuals, a plumbing fixture, site, building, facility, or portion thereof that can be approached, entered, and/or used.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The amount a pressure, temperature, liquid level, or differential pressure deviates higher from a set value.
mcqs.explanation_long: Accumulation: The amount a pressure, temperature, liquid level, or differential pressure deviates higher from a set value.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A container in which fluid is stored under pressure as a source of power.
mcqs.explanation_long: Accumulator: A container in which fluid is stored under pressure as a source of power.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A fluid with a pH lower than 7.0.
mcqs.explanation_long: Acid: A fluid with a pH lower than 7.0.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe venting an acid-waste system.
mcqs.explanation_long: Acid vent: A pipe venting an acid-waste system.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A pipe that conveys liquid waste matter having a pH of less than 7.0.
mcqs.explanation_long: Acid waste: A pipe that conveys liquid waste matter having a pH of less than 7.0.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Sewage sediment, rich in destructive bacteria, that can be used to break down fresh sewage quickly.
mcqs.explanation_long: Activated sludge: Sewage sediment, rich in destructive bacteria, that can be used to break down fresh sewage quickly.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A movable component of a valve that when operated causes the closure element to move.
mcqs.explanation_long: Actuator: A movable component of a valve that when operated causes the closure element to move.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: With reference to inlet conditions, the volume rate of air compressed and delivered at the dis charge point.
mcqs.explanation_long: Actual capacity: With reference to inlet conditions, the volume rate of air compressed and delivered at the dis charge point.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any fitting that mates or connects two pipes or fittings of different size, material, or design.
mcqs.explanation_long: Adapter fitting: Any fitting that mates or connects two pipes or fittings of different size, material, or design.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A substance capable of holding two or more objects together by attaching to their surfaces.
mcqs.explanation_long: Adhesive: A substance capable of holding two or more objects together by attaching to their surfaces.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Air compression that allows an increase of temperature in the air.
mcqs.explanation_long: Adiabatic compression: Air compression that allows an increase of temperature in the air.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The surface retention or adhesion of a gas on the surface of a solid .
mcqs.explanation_long: Adsorption: The surface retention or adhesion of a gas on the surface of a solid .

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Also called the authority having jurisdiction, the individual official, board, department, entity, or agency established and authorized by a state, county, city, or other political subdivision created by law to administer and enforce the provisions of a particular code as adapted or amended.
mcqs.explanation_long: Administrative authority: Also called the authority having jurisdiction, the individual official, board, department, entity, or agency established and authorized by a state, county, city, or other political subdivision created by law to administer and enforce the provisions of a particular code as adapted or amended.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: An artificial method of bringing a liquid into direct contact with air.
mcqs.explanation_long: Aeration: An artificial method of bringing a liquid into direct contact with air.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Living or active only in the presence of free oxygen.
mcqs.explanation_long: Aerobic: Living or active only in the presence of free oxygen.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A small vapor particle suspended in air.
mcqs.explanation_long: Aerosol: A small vapor particle suspended in air.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device used to lower the temperature of a gas after the compression process.
mcqs.explanation_long: Aftercooler: A device used to lower the temperature of a gas after the compression process.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The effect of exposing material to a specific environment for an extended period of time.
mcqs.explanation_long: Aging: The effect of exposing material to a specific environment for an extended period of time.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A one-way valve designed to open and allow air to enter a drainage system when negative pressures are present without extending to the outside air and then positively close by gravity.
mcqs.explanation_long: Air-admittance valve: A one-way valve designed to open and allow air to enter a drainage system when negative pressures are present without extending to the outside air and then positively close by gravity.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device, either made up of fittings or manufactured, installed on a piping system for the purpose of attenuating a pressure surge resulting from water hammer.
mcqs.explanation_long: Air chamber: A device, either made up of fittings or manufactured, installed on a piping system for the purpose of attenuating a pressure surge resulting from water hammer.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Air at any pressure greater than atmospheric pressure.
mcqs.explanation_long: Air, compressed: Air at any pressure greater than atmospheric pressure.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Air subject only to ambient atmospheric conditions.
mcqs.explanation_long: Air, free: Air subject only to ambient atmospheric conditions.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The unobstructed vertical distance between the end of a pipe and the flood level of a plumbing fixture or receptacle.
mcqs.explanation_long: Air gap: The unobstructed vertical distance between the end of a pipe and the flood level of a plumbing fixture or receptacle.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Sometimes called vapor lock, a condition in which a gas is entrapped between two liquid surfaces in a conduit causing a stoppage or impediment.
mcqs.explanation_long: Air lock: Sometimes called vapor lock, a condition in which a gas is entrapped between two liquid surfaces in a conduit causing a stoppage or impediment.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Air having a temperature of 60°F (15.6°C), with a pressure of 14.70 psia (101.4 kPa) and 0 percent humidity. These figures may be different outside the United States.
mcqs.explanation_long: Air, standard: Air having a temperature of 60°F (15.6°C), with a pressure of 14.70 psia (101.4 kPa) and 0 percent humidity. These figures may be different outside the United States.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A test using compressed air applied to a plumbing system for the purpose of determining a leak.
mcqs.explanation_long: Air test: A test using compressed air applied to a plumbing system for the purpose of determining a leak.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Any audible or visible signal indicating the existence of any condition or operation outside a preset normal.
mcqs.explanation_long: Alarm: Any audible or visible signal indicating the existence of any condition or operation outside a preset normal.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A check valve, equipped with a signaling device, that will annunciate a remote alarm when a sprinkler head is discharging.
mcqs.explanation_long: Alarm check valve: A check valve, equipped with a signaling device, that will annunciate a remote alarm when a sprinkler head is discharging.
mcqs.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Alarm check valve

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A fluid with a pH of 7.0 or higher.
mcqs.explanation_long: Alkali: A fluid with a pH of 7.0 or higher.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A substance composed of two or more metals or a metal and nonmetal intimately united, usually fused together and dissolving in each other when molten.
mcqs.explanation_long: Alloy: A substance composed of two or more metals or a metal and nonmetal intimately united, usually fused together and dissolving in each other when molten.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A metallic pipe composed of two or more metals.
mcqs.explanation_long: Alloy pipe: A metallic pipe composed of two or more metals.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: The average or mean temperature of the surrounding air where a reading is taken.
mcqs.explanation_long: Ambient temperature: The average or mean temperature of the surrounding air where a reading is taken.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A type of screw thread commonly used on pipe and fittings.
mcqs.explanation_long: American standard pipe thread: A type of screw thread commonly used on pipe and fittings.
mcqs.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | American standard pipe thread

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device that magnifies the input signal using power other than that from the signal itself.
mcqs.explanation_long: Amplifier: A device that magnifies the input signal using power other than that from the signal itself.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A measurement of the distance between the highest to the lowest excursion of a variable or physical motion.
mcqs.explanation_long: Amplitude: A measurement of the distance between the highest to the lowest excursion of a variable or physical motion.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: (re: bacteria) Living or active in the absence of free oxygen.
mcqs.explanation_long: Anaerobic: (re: bacteria) Living or active in the absence of free oxygen.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: Separation and measurements of component parts.
mcqs.explanation_long: Analysis: Separation and measurements of component parts.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: A device used to fasten or secure pipes to a building or structure.
mcqs.explanation_long: Anchor: A device used to fasten or secure pipes to a building or structure.

mcqs.question_text: Which plumbing term matches this definition: In a pipe, the angle between radial lines from the beginning and end of the bend to the center.
mcqs.explanation_long: Angle of bend: In a pipe, the angle between radial lines from the beginning and end of the bend to the center.
mcqs.source_ref: ASPE PLUMBING TERMINOLOGY.pdf | Angle of bend
